params.out: Output unit selector: rayl (default) returns SI Rayl (kg/m2/s), mrayl returns MRayl (Z/1e6); other values raise ValueError.

biot_coefficient.returns: Dimensionless Biot-Willis effective-stress coefficient alpha = 1 - K_dry/K_mineral as a numpy float64 array.

bowers_pore_pressure.returns: Bowers (1995) pore pressure Pp = overburden - effective stress as a numpy float64 array, in psi (field units).

brittleness_rickman.returns: Rickman (2008) brittleness index, the average of the min-max normalised Young's modulus and (inverted) Poisson's ratio terms, as a numpy float64 array; nominally 0-1 (or 0-100 when percent=True), not clipped for out-of-range inputs.

eaton_pore_pressure.returns: Eaton pore pressure Pp = S_ov - (S_ov - P_hydro)*ratio**exponent as a numpy float64 array, in the same pressure unit as overburden and hydrostatic.

params.sigma_total: Total stress in Pa (or any consistent pressure unit shared with pp).

params.k_fluid: Bulk modulus of the pore fluid in Pa.
params.k_mineral: Bulk modulus of the solid mineral grains in Pa.
params.k_sat: Saturated-rock bulk modulus in Pa; keyword-only to avoid the k_dry/k_sat argument collision.
params.phi: Porosity as a volume fraction between 0 and 1.

gassmann_kdry.returns: Gassmann dry-frame bulk modulus K_dry (inverse fluid substitution) as a numpy float64 array, in the same units as the input moduli.

params.k_dry: Dry-frame (drained rock skeleton) bulk modulus in Pa; keyword-only to avoid the k_dry/k_sat argument collision.
params.k_fluid: Bulk modulus of the pore fluid in Pa.
params.k_mineral: Bulk modulus of the solid mineral grains in Pa.

gassmann_ksat.returns: Gassmann saturated-rock bulk modulus K_sat as a numpy float64 array, in the same units as the input moduli (Pa with SI inputs).

params.rho: Pore-fluid density in kg/m3; defaults to 1000.0 (fresh water).

hydrostatic_pressure.returns: Hydrostatic pressure P = rho*g*depth as a numpy float64 array, in Pa with SI inputs.

params.sH: Maximum horizontal stress in the working pressure unit.
params.sh: Minimum horizontal stress, same pressure unit as sH.
params.theta_deg: Angular position around the borehole wall, in degrees measured from the sH azimuth.

kirsch_hoop_stress.returns: Kirsch tangential (hoop) stress at the borehole wall, (sH + sh) - 2*(sH - sh)*cos(2*theta) - Pw, as a numpy float64 array in the input pressure unit.

params.rho: Bulk density in kg/m3.

lame_from_velocity.returns: Tuple (lambda, mu) of numpy float64 arrays: first Lame parameter lambda = rho*Vp**2 - 2*mu and shear modulus mu = rho*Vs**2, in Pa with SI inputs.

params.c11: VTI stiffness constant C11, Pa under the module convention.
params.c33: VTI stiffness constant C33, same units as c11.
params.c44: VTI stiffness constant C44, same units as c11.

mannie2_c66.returns: Modified-ANNIE-2 estimate of the shear stiffness C66 = C44*(1 + k*(C11 - C33)/C33) as a numpy float64 array, in the units of the inputs.

params.c33: VTI stiffness constant C33, Pa under the module convention.
params.c44: VTI stiffness constant C44, same units as c33.
params.c66: VTI stiffness constant C66, same units as c33.
params.kp: Dimensionless modified-ANNIE scaling factor applied to 2*(C66 - C44); default 1.0 recovers plain ANNIE.

mannie_c11.returns: Modified-ANNIE estimate of the stiffness constant C11 = kp*2*(C66 - C44) + C33 as a numpy float64 array, in the units of the inputs.

params.c11: VTI stiffness constant C11, Pa under the module convention.
params.c66: VTI stiffness constant C66, same units as c11.
params.k: Dimensionless modified-ANNIE scaling factor applied to (C11 - 2*C66); default 1.0 recovers plain ANNIE.

mannie_c13.returns: Modified-ANNIE estimate of the stiffness constant C13 = k*(C11 - 2*C66) as a numpy float64 array, in the units of the inputs.

params.e: Young's modulus in the same pressure unit as the stresses; default 0.0 disables the poroelastic strain terms.
params.eps_H: Dimensionless tectonic strain in the maximum-horizontal-stress direction; default 0.0.
params.eps_h: Dimensionless tectonic strain in the minimum-horizontal-stress direction; default 0.0.
params.nu: Dimensionless Poisson's ratio of the formation.
params.pp: Pore pressure, same pressure unit as sv.
params.sv: Overburden (vertical) stress in the working pressure unit.
params.tectonic: Constant additive tectonic stress term, same pressure unit as the stresses; default 0.0.

min_horizontal_stress.returns: Minimum horizontal stress sh = nu/(1-nu)*(Sv - biot*Pp) + biot*Pp + E/(1-nu**2)*(eps_h + nu*eps_H) + tectonic as a numpy float64 array, in the input pressure unit.

params.rho: Bulk density of the rock in kg/m3.

moduli_from_velocity.returns: Tuple (K, G) of numpy float64 arrays: bulk modulus K = rho*Vp**2 - 4/3*G and shear modulus G = rho*Vs**2, in Pa with SI inputs. The order is fixed as (K, G).

params.depth: Vertical depth below surface in m.
params.g: Gravitational acceleration in m/s2; defaults to standard gravity 9.80665.
params.rho_bulk: Average or per-sample overburden bulk density in kg/m3.

overburden_stress.returns: Overburden (vertical) stress sigma_v = rho_bulk*g*depth as a numpy float64 array, in Pa with SI inputs.

params.g: Shear modulus in Pa (same units as k; only the ratio matters).

poisson_from_kg.returns: Dimensionless Poisson's ratio nu = (3K - 2G)/(2*(3K + G)) as a numpy float64 array.

params.vs: Shear (S-wave) velocity in m/s (same units as vp; only the ratio matters).

poisson_from_velocity.returns: Dimensionless Poisson's ratio (Vp**2 - 2*Vs**2)/(2*(Vp**2 - Vs**2)) as a numpy float64 array.

pwave_modulus.returns: P-wave (constrained) modulus M = K + 4/3*G as a numpy float64 array, in the same units as the inputs (Pa under the module convention).

params.z1: Acoustic impedance of the incident-side medium, any consistent impedance unit.
params.z2: Acoustic impedance of the transmitted-side medium, same units as z1.

reflection_coefficient.returns: Dimensionless normal-incidence amplitude reflection coefficient (Z2 - Z1)/(Z2 + Z1) as a numpy float64 array, between -1 and 1.

params.pp: Pore pressure, same pressure unit as sh.
params.sH: Maximum horizontal stress, same pressure unit as sh.
params.sh: Minimum horizontal stress in the working pressure unit.

reopening_pressure.returns: Fracture reopening pressure 3*sh - sH - Pp (breakdown pressure with zero tensile strength) as a numpy float64 array, in the input pressure unit.

params.f: Volume fractions of the mixture components arranged along the mixing axis, expected to sum to one.
params.m: Component moduli (or other property values) arranged along the mixing axis, all in the same units.

reuss.returns: Reuss (harmonic) lower-bound modulus 1/sum(f_i/m_i) as a numpy float64 array with the mixing axis reduced, in the units of m.

params.v_fast: Velocity of the fast shear wave in m/s.
params.v_slow: Velocity of the slow shear wave, same units as v_fast.

shear_wave_splitting.returns: Dimensionless shear-wave splitting (anisotropy) fraction (V_fast - V_slow)/V_fast as a numpy float64 array.

params.pp: Pore pressure, same pressure unit as pr.
params.pr: Measured fracture reopening pressure in the working pressure unit.
params.sh: Minimum horizontal stress, same pressure unit as pr.

shmax_from_reopening.returns: Maximum horizontal stress sH = 3*sh - Pr - Pp inverted from the reopening relation, as a numpy float64 array in the input pressure unit.

params.dt_unit: Output slowness unit: us/ft (default) returns 0.3048*1e6/v, us/m returns 1e6/v; any other value raises ValueError.

slowness_from_velocity.returns: Acoustic slowness as a numpy float64 array, in us/ft or us/m according to dt_unit.

params.degrees: If True (default) the angle is returned in degrees; if False, in radians.
params.v1: Wave velocity in the first (incident) medium, in m/s; when v1 is the slower medium the result is the critical angle.
params.v2: Wave velocity in the second medium, same units as v1.

snell_angle.returns: Snell critical/refraction angle arcsin(V1/V2) as a numpy float64 array, in degrees by default or radians when degrees=False.

params.rho: Bulk density in kg/m3.
params.v: Wave velocity in m/s; using Vp yields the P-wave modulus M, using Vs the shear modulus.

stiffness_from_velocity.returns: Stiffness coefficient C = rho*V**2 as a numpy float64 array, in Pa with SI inputs.

params.c13: VTI stiffness constant C13, Pa under the module convention.
params.c33: VTI stiffness constant C33 (vertical P-wave stiffness), same units as c13.
params.c44: VTI stiffness constant C44 (vertical shear stiffness), same units as c13.

thomsen_delta.returns: Dimensionless Thomsen delta parameter ((C13 + C44)**2 - (C33 - C44)**2)/(2*C33*(C33 - C44)) as a numpy float64 array.

params.c11: VTI stiffness constant C11 (horizontal P-wave stiffness), Pa under the module convention.
params.c33: VTI stiffness constant C33 (vertical P-wave stiffness), same units as c11.

thomsen_epsilon.returns: Dimensionless Thomsen P-wave anisotropy parameter epsilon = (C11 - C33)/(2*C33) as a numpy float64 array.

params.c44: VTI stiffness constant C44 (vertical shear stiffness), same units as c66.
params.c66: VTI stiffness constant C66 (horizontally polarized shear stiffness), Pa under the module convention.

thomsen_gamma.returns: Dimensionless Thomsen S-wave anisotropy parameter gamma = (C66 - C44)/(2*C44) as a numpy float64 array.

params.z1: Acoustic impedance of the incident-side medium, any consistent impedance unit.
params.z2: Acoustic impedance of the transmitted-side medium, same units as z1.

transmission_energy.returns: Dimensionless fraction of incident energy transmitted across the interface, 1 - R**2 with R the normal-incidence reflection coefficient, as a numpy float64 array.

params.rho: Bulk density of the rock in kg/m3.

velocity_from_moduli.returns: Tuple (Vp, Vs) of numpy float64 arrays: compressional velocity sqrt((K + 4/3*G)/rho) and shear velocity sqrt(G/rho), in m/s with SI inputs.

velocity_from_slowness.returns: Velocity in m/s as a numpy float64 array.

params.axis: Integer array axis along which the components are mixed and summed; defaults to -1, the last axis.
params.f: Volume fractions of the mixture components arranged along the mixing axis, expected to sum to one.
params.m: Component moduli (or other property values) arranged along the mixing axis, all in the same units.

voigt.returns: Voigt (arithmetic) upper-bound modulus sum(f_i*m_i) as a numpy float64 array with the mixing axis reduced, in the units of m.

params.axis: Integer array axis along which the components are mixed; defaults to -1, the last axis.
params.f: Volume fractions of the mixture components arranged along the mixing axis, expected to sum to one.
params.m: Component moduli arranged along the mixing axis, all in the same units.

voigt_reuss_hill.returns: Voigt-Reuss-Hill average modulus 0.5*(Voigt + Reuss) as a numpy float64 array with the mixing axis reduced, in the units of m.

params.method: Empirical correlation selector: castagna_ls (default, Castagna limestone quadratic), pickett_ls (Vp/1.9), pickett_dol (Vp/1.8), carroll (power law), or brocher (Brocher 2005 fourth-order polynomial); other values raise ValueError.

vs_from_vp.returns: Estimated shear (S-wave) velocity Vs in km/s as a numpy float64 array.

params.c11: VTI stiffness constant C11, Pa under the module convention.
params.c12: Optional VTI stiffness constant C12, same units as c11; when None (default) it is computed as C11 - 2*C66.
params.c13: VTI stiffness constant C13, same units as c11.
params.c33: VTI stiffness constant C33, same units as c11.
params.c44: VTI stiffness constant C44, same units as c11.
params.c66: VTI stiffness constant C66, same units as c11.

vti_engineering_moduli.returns: Dict of numpy float64 arrays with keys Ev, Eh, nu_v, nu_h, Gvh, Ghh: vertical and horizontal Young's moduli, vertical and horizontal Poisson's ratios (dimensionless), and the shear moduli Gvh = C44 and Ghh = C66, moduli in the units of the stiffness inputs.

params.axis: Integer array axis along which the fluid phases are mixed; defaults to -1, the last axis.
params.moduli: Bulk moduli of the individual fluid phases arranged along the mixing axis, in Pa with SI inputs.
params.saturations: Fluid-phase saturations (fractions summing to one) arranged along the mixing axis.

params.rho: Bulk density in kg/m3.

youngs_poisson_dynamic.returns: Tuple (E, nu) of numpy float64 arrays: dynamic Young's modulus E in Pa with SI inputs, and dimensionless dynamic Poisson's ratio nu.

params.section_azimuth_deg: Angle beta in degrees between the cross-section direction and the true-dip direction.
params.true_dip_deg: True dip of the plane in degrees.

apparent_dip.returns: Apparent dip seen in the section, from tan(apparent) = tan(true)*cos(beta), in degrees as a scalar float.

params.azimuth_deg: Borehole-wall azimuth values phi in degrees at which the bed depth trace is evaluated.
params.dip_azimuth_deg: Dip azimuth in degrees, the azimuth at which the sinusoid reaches its deepest point.
params.dip_deg: True dip of the planar bed in degrees; the sinusoid amplitude is radius*tan(dip).
params.radius: Borehole radius, in the same length units as z0; scales the sinusoid amplitude.
params.z0: Sinusoid offset, the bed depth at its mean level, in the same length units as radius.

bed_sinusoid.returns: Depth trace z(phi) = z0 - radius*tan(dip)*cos(phi - dip_azimuth) on the unrolled borehole wall, as a numpy float64 array matching azimuth_deg, in the length units of z0 and radius.

params.image: Grey-level image of any shape (flattened internally) to partition into intensity classes.
params.thresholds: Ascending threshold values [t0, t1, ...] in the image's intensity units defining the class boundaries.

class_fractions.returns: Numpy float64 array of len(thresholds)+1 volume fractions: mean(img < t0), the fractions between consecutive thresholds, and mean(img >= t_last), summing to one; e.g. pore/clay/grain from two thresholds (dark = pore convention).

params.amplitude: Fitted sinusoid amplitude, e.g. from fit_sinusoid; may be in depth samples if sample_spacing converts it to length.
params.radius: Borehole radius, in the same length units as amplitude*sample_spacing.
params.sample_spacing: Depth increment per sample (default 1.0) converting an amplitude expressed in samples to length units.

dip_from_amplitude.returns: True dip angle arctan(amplitude*sample_spacing/radius) in degrees, as a scalar float.

params.points_enz: Array of shape (n, 3) of (Easting, Northing, TVD) coordinates of points lying on the plane to fit.

fit_plane.returns: Tuple of two floats (dip_deg, dip_azimuth_deg): the plane dip arccos(|n_z|) in degrees, and the dip azimuth atan2(n_E, n_N) mod 360 in degrees clockwise from North.

params.azimuth_deg: Azimuth samples in degrees corresponding to the picked depths z.
params.mask: Optional boolean array selecting the valid samples to include in the least-squares fit.
params.z: Picked bed-trace depth values along the azimuth axis, in any consistent length or sample units.

fit_sinusoid.returns: Tuple of three floats (z0, amplitude, phase_deg): the fitted sinusoid offset, the amplitude hypot(c1, c2) in the units of z, and the phase atan2(c2, c1) in degrees.

params.image: 2-D grey-level image indexed [depth, azimuth] (rows = depth, columns = azimuth), quantised internally to the requested grey levels by min-max scaling.

params.offset: Pixel displacement (drow, dcol) between co-occurring pairs (default (0, 1), horizontal neighbours).
params.symmetric: If True (default), each pixel pair is counted in both directions, making the matrix symmetric.

glcm.returns: Normalised grey-level co-occurrence matrix of shape (levels, levels) as a numpy float64 array whose entries sum to one (left all-zero if no pairs were counted).

params.p: Normalised (sum-to-one) square grey-level co-occurrence matrix, e.g. the output of glcm.

glcm_features.returns: Dict of scalar floats with keys 'contrast', 'energy', and 'correlation', the Haralick texture features of the GLCM; correlation is 0.0 when either marginal variance is zero.

params.bins: Number of histogram bins spanning [min, max] of the image (default 256).
params.image: Grey-level image of any shape (flattened internally), thresholded on its native min-max data range.

otsu_threshold.returns: Otsu threshold as a scalar float in the image's native intensity units, the centre of the histogram bin that maximises the between-class variance.

params.phase: Boolean (or truthy) mask flagging the voxels/pixels occupied by the fluid phase of interest.
params.pore: Boolean (or truthy) mask flagging the pore voxels/pixels.

phase_saturation.returns: Saturation of the phase within the pore space, count(phase AND pore)/count(pore), as a dimensionless scalar float fraction.

params.pore: Boolean (or truthy) segmentation mask flagging pore voxels/pixels.

porosity_from_mask.returns: Porosity as the fraction of voxels flagged pore, pore.sum()/pore.size, a dimensionless scalar float in [0, 1].

params.image: 2-D grey-level image indexed [depth, azimuth] (rows = depth, columns = azimuth).

sobel_gradient.returns: Tuple of three numpy float64 arrays (gx, gy, magnitude), each with the image's shape: the horizontal (column) derivative, the vertical (row) derivative, and sqrt(gx^2 + gy^2); the one-pixel border of gx and gy is zero.

params.clip: Optional (lo, hi) tuple clipping the resulting Sw; default None (unclipped).
params.lam: Brooks-Corey pore-size-distribution index lambda; sources using the reciprocal 1/N convention must pass lam=1.0/N (the two are not interchangeable).
params.pc: Capillary pressure, in the same pressure unit as pc_entry.
params.pc_entry: Entry (displacement) pressure Pe, in the same unit as pc.

brooks_corey_sw.returns: Brooks-Corey drainage water saturation Sw = Swirr + (1-Swirr)*(Pe/Pc)**lam (v/v fraction), set to 1.0 at and below the entry pressure. Numpy float64 array broadcast over the inputs.

params.g: Gravitational acceleration in m/s2; defaults to standard gravity 9.80665.
params.height: Height above the free-water level in m.

buoyancy_pc.returns: Buoyancy capillary pressure Pc = delta_rho * g * h in Pa. Numpy float64 array broadcast over the inputs.

params.theta_deg: Contact angle in degrees; default 0.0. The signed cosine is always used (no absolute option).

params.delta_rho: Density difference between the two fluids in kg/m3.

params.g: Gravitational acceleration in m/s2; defaults to standard gravity 9.80665.

params.pc: Capillary pressure measured in the source (e.g. laboratory air-mercury) fluid system; the output keeps its pressure unit.
params.sigma_from: Interfacial tension of the source fluid system in N/m.
params.sigma_to: Interfacial tension of the target (e.g. reservoir gas-brine) system in N/m.
params.theta_from_deg: Contact angle of the source fluid system in degrees.
params.theta_to_deg: Contact angle of the target system in degrees.

pc_convert_system.returns: Capillary pressure converted to the target system by the sigma*cos(theta) ratio, Pc * (sigma_to*cos(theta_to))/(sigma_from*cos(theta_from)), in the same pressure unit as pc. Numpy float64 array broadcast over the inputs.

params.absolute: If True (default), uses |cos(theta)|; if False, uses the signed cosine.
params.k: Permeability expressed as an area (e.g. m2).
params.phi: Porosity (v/v fraction).
params.sigma: Interfacial tension of the target fluid system in N/m (sets the output pressure unit).
params.theta_deg: Contact angle of the target system in degrees; default 0.0.

pc_from_leverett_j.returns: Capillary pressure Pc = J*sigma*cos(theta)/sqrt(k/phi), in Pa with SI inputs; the inverse of leverett_j. Numpy float64 array broadcast over the inputs.

params.bv_inf: Thomeer asymptote Bv_inf: the bulk volume occupied by mercury at infinite pressure, setting the unit/scale of the result.
params.g: Thomeer pore geometrical factor G; its numerical value depends on log_base (log10 and natural-log G values differ by ln(10) = 2.303 and are not interchangeable).
params.log_base: Base of the logarithm in the hyperbola exponent; default 10.0 (log10 convention), pass e for the natural-log convention.
params.pc: Capillary pressure, in the same pressure unit as pd.
params.pd: Displacement (entry) pressure Pd, in the same unit as pc.

thomeer_shg.returns: Thomeer mercury bulk-volume saturation Shg = Bv_inf * exp(-G / log_base(Pc/Pd)), in the same unit as bv_inf, and 0.0 at and below Pd. Numpy float64 array broadcast over the inputs.

params.absolute: If True (default), uses |cos(theta)|, folding the mercury contact angle's negative sign into a positive result; if False, uses the signed cosine.
params.theta_deg: Contact angle in degrees; default 0.0 (mercury sources use 140 or 180).

washburn_diameter.returns: Pore-throat diameter d = 4*sigma*cos(theta)/Pc in m (with SI inputs); the diameter dialect of washburn_radius. Numpy float64 array broadcast over the inputs.

params.absolute: If True (default), uses |cos(theta)| (MICP convention); if False, uses the signed cosine.

washburn_radius.returns: Pore-throat radius r = 2*sigma*cos(theta)/Pc in m (with SI inputs); the inverse of young_laplace_pc. Numpy float64 array broadcast over the inputs.

params.absolute: If True (default), uses |cos(theta)| (the MICP convention, keeping Pc positive); if False, uses the signed cosine for physical sign conventions.
params.theta_deg: Contact angle in degrees; default 0.0 gives the pure spherical-meniscus jump 2*sigma/r.

young_laplace_pc.returns: Capillary entry pressure Pc = 2*sigma*cos(theta)/r in Pa (with SI inputs). Numpy float64 array broadcast over the inputs.

params.*fracs: One or more fraction arrays (e.g. saturations Sw, So, Sg) that are summed elementwise; at least one is required.
params.target: Value the fractions should sum to (default 1.0).

closure_residual.returns: Numpy float array of the closure residual sum(fracs) - target; zero where the fractions close exactly.

params.replace: Replacement rule for flagged spikes: 'interp' (default) linear interpolation from surviving samples, 'median' the global median of the input, 'nan' NaN.
params.side: 'both' (default), 'upper', or 'lower', restricting which tail of the distribution counts as spikes.
params.threshold: MAD multiplier of the spike criterion: samples further than threshold*MAD*1.4826 from the median are spikes (default 3.0).
params.x: 1-D curve to be despiked.

despike.returns: Numpy float copy of x with the flagged spikes replaced according to the replace rule; an unchanged copy if no spikes are found.

params.index: Optional sample coordinates (e.g. depths) used as the interpolation abscissa; defaults to arange(len(x)).
params.x: 1-D curve containing NaN gaps to be filled.

impute_gaps.returns: Numpy float copy of x with NaN runs filled by linear interpolation between surrounding valid samples; leading and trailing NaNs are held flat at the nearest valid value. Raises ValueError if x has no finite values.

params.k: Whisker multiplier for 'iqr': outliers fall outside (Q1 - k*IQR, Q3 + k*IQR); default 1.5, ignored by the other methods.
params.method: Detection method: 'zscore' (default) population z-score, 'iqr' Tukey interquartile whiskers, 'mad' robust median-absolute-deviation criterion.
params.side: 'both' (default) flags both tails, 'upper' only high exceedances, 'lower' only low ones.
params.threshold: Cutoff for 'zscore' (|z| > threshold) and 'mad' (further than threshold*MAD*1.4826 from the median); default 3.0, ignored by 'iqr'.
params.x: Input samples screened for outliers.

outlier_mask.returns: Boolean numpy array the same shape as x, True where a sample is flagged as an outlier by the chosen criterion (strict comparisons).

params.a: First set of measurements being compared.
params.b: Second set of measurements being compared.

relative_discrepancy.returns: Numpy float array of the dimensionless relative discrepancy 2*|a - b| / (a + b), the absolute difference divided by the mean of the two values.

params.axis: Axis along which the fractions are summed and normalized (default -1, the last axis).
params.fracs: Array of component fractions (e.g. mineral volumes or oxides) to be rescaled.

renormalize.returns: Numpy float array of the fractions divided by their sum along axis so they sum to one; a zero sum yields NaN/inf rather than a guessed composition.

params.atol: Absolute tolerance of the np.isclose sentinel match (default 1e-8).
params.rtol: Relative tolerance of the np.isclose sentinel match (default 1e-5), so float round-trips of a sentinel still match.
params.sentinels: Sequence of sentinel values to replace; defaults to the common LAS/DLIS set (-999.0, -999.25, -9999.0).
params.x: Input log/curve values that may contain no-data sentinel values.

sentinels_to_nan.returns: Numpy float copy of x with every value matching a sentinel (within the isclose tolerances) replaced by NaN.

params.curve: 1-D log curve scanned for bed boundaries.
params.threshold: Detection cutoff on the metric |gradient|*sqrt(moving variance); None (default) uses the adaptive value mean + 2*std of the metric.
params.window: Centered window length in samples of the moving variance in the detection metric; edge samples are left 0 (default 11).

detect_bed_boundaries.returns: Integer numpy array of sample indices where the metric is a local maximum above the threshold, interpreted as bed-boundary positions.

params.size: Odd side length of the square median window (default 3).

median_filter2d.returns: Numpy float array of the median-filtered image, same shape as img, computed over the size-by-size neighborhood with edge-replicated padding.

params.center: True (default) uses the symmetric window centered on each sample; False uses the causal trailing window ending at the sample.
params.stat: Statistic to compute: 'mean' (default), 'std', 'var' (population, ddof=0), 'mad' mean absolute deviation about the window mean, or 'cv' std/mean (0 where the mean is 0).
params.window: Window length in samples; windows shrink at the edges rather than padding.
params.x: 1-D curve over which the moving statistic is computed.

moving_stat.returns: Numpy float array, same length as x, holding the moving-window statistic evaluated at every sample.

params.kind: Filter type: 'boxcar' (default) centered moving average, 'gaussian' normalized Gaussian kernel, 'median' running median with edge-replicated padding.
params.sigma: Gaussian kernel standard deviation in samples; required when kind='gaussian' and ignored otherwise.
params.window: Filter length in samples: the boxcar length (window <= 1 returns a copy) or the odd running-median window; unused for kind='gaussian'. Default 5.
params.x: 1-D curve to be smoothed.

smooth.returns: Numpy float array of the smoothed curve, same length as x; boxcar and Gaussian smoothing use zero-padded np.convolve(mode='same') edges, the median filter uses edge-replicated padding.

params.dz: Grid (sample) spacing of x, in the same length units as fwhm.
params.fwhm: Full width at half maximum of the Gaussian tool vertical response (the tool aperture), in the same length units as dz.
params.x: Fine-scale curve (e.g. core-measured property versus depth) to be blurred to logging-tool resolution.

tool_response.returns: Numpy float array of the curve convolved with the unit-normalized Gaussian tool response (sigma = fwhm/(2*sqrt(2*ln 2)), np.convolve mode='same' with zero-padded edges), same length as x.

params.window: Odd window length in samples, centered on each depth sample (default 11).
params.x: 1-D curve of length n, or 2-D (n, d) matrix of d curves, to be expanded into sliding windows.

window_features.returns: Numpy float feature matrix: shape (n, window) for a 1-D input or (n, d*window) for a 2-D input, where row i is the edge-padded window centered on sample i.

params.target_mean: Desired mean of the output, in the curve's units.
params.target_std: Desired population standard deviation (ddof=0) of the output, in the curve's units.
params.x: Curve values to transform.

match_moments.returns: Float64 array of the affine transform (x - mean(x))/std(x)*target_std + target_mean, having exactly the target mean and population std; a constant input divides by zero (producing inf/NaN) rather than guessing a scale.

params.in_hi: Input-well high endpoint; if None (default) it is derived from x using pct (or the maximum when pct is None).
params.in_lo: Input-well low endpoint; if None (default) it is derived from x using pct (or the minimum when pct is None).
params.pct: Pair of (low, high) percentiles used to derive omitted endpoints from x; default (5.0, 95.0), the Shier convention; None selects min/max instead (image-histogram convention).
params.ref_hi: Reference-well high endpoint, in curve units, that the input-well high endpoint is mapped to.
params.ref_lo: Reference-well low endpoint, in curve units, that the input-well low endpoint is mapped to.
params.x: Input-well curve values to normalize (e.g. a gamma-ray log or image histogram).

normalize_to_reference.returns: Float64 array of x affinely mapped onto the reference endpoints: out = ref_lo + (ref_hi - ref_lo)*(x - in_lo)/(in_hi - in_lo). No clipping is applied, so values outside the input endpoints extrapolate beyond the reference range.

params.rng: Numpy random Generator or integer seed controlling the draw; None (default) uses the source article's deterministic default_rng(0).
params.sigma_fraction: Noise standard deviation expressed as a fraction of the curve's mean absolute level: sigma = sigma_fraction * mean(|x|).
params.x: Clean curve to which the synthetic noise is added.

add_gaussian_noise.returns: Float64 array of the same shape as x with zero-mean Gaussian noise of a single scalar standard deviation added to every sample.

params.axis: Axis along which consecutive groups of n samples are averaged; default -1 (the last axis).
params.n: Number of consecutive samples averaged into each block; must be at least 1.
params.x: Array of repeat measurements to be stacked.

block_stack.returns: Float64 array with the stacking axis reduced to size // n block means; a trailing partial block shorter than n is dropped. Other axes are unchanged.

params.guard: Small constant added to the measured noise power (e.g. 1e-30, the VMD-paper convention) to guard against a zero denominator; default 0.0.
params.noise: Noise record or residual whose power is mean(noise**2) + guard; give exactly one of noise or noise_std.
params.noise_std: Known noise standard deviation; the noise power is noise_std**2; give exactly one of noise or noise_std.
params.signal: Signal record whose power is computed as mean(signal**2).

snr_db.returns: Scalar float: the signal-to-noise ratio in dB, 10*log10(signal power / noise power); returns inf when the noise power is exactly zero.

params.n: Number of repeat measurements stacked (scalar or array).

stack_gain.returns: Amplitude SNR improvement factor sqrt(n) from stacking, dimensionless, as a float64 numpy array.

params.level_range: (low, high) range from which each bed level is drawn uniformly (default (20.0, 150.0)).
params.n: Number of samples in the generated log.
params.n_beds: Number of constant-level beds (default 8); must be at least 1 and small enough for the interior margin.
params.noise: Standard deviation of the additive Gaussian noise on the curve (default 2.0).
params.rng: Random source: a numpy Generator, an integer seed, or None for the deterministic default_rng(0).

blocky_log.returns: Tuple (curve, bounds): the length-n noisy blocky log as a numpy float array, and a sorted integer array of the n_beds - 1 interior boundary indices drawn without replacement away from the ends.

params.n_features: Number of dark disks (radii 2 to 7 pixels) stamped at uniform random centers.
params.rng: Random source: a numpy Generator, an integer seed, or None for the deterministic default_rng(0).
params.size: Side length in pixels of the square output image.

disk_image.returns: Tuple (image, mask): a (size, size) numpy float grey image with a bright noisy background (about 0.6) and dark disks (about 0.15), clipped to [0, 1], and the boolean (size, size) mask that is True on disk (pore) pixels.

params.amps: Peak amplitude of each Gaussian component.
params.axis: Spectrum abscissa values (e.g. NMR T2 times or pore sizes) at which the spectrum is evaluated.
params.centres: Center position of each Gaussian component, in axis units (converted to log10 when log_axis is True).
params.log_axis: If True (default), components are Gaussian in log10 of the axis (widths in decades); if False, in the raw axis values.
params.noise: Standard deviation of Gaussian noise added when greater than 0; the noisy spectrum is then clipped at zero (default 0.0).
params.rng: Random source used only when noise > 0: a numpy Generator, an integer seed, or None for the deterministic default_rng(0).
params.widths: Standard deviation of each component: in decades when log_axis is True, otherwise in raw axis units.

gaussian_mixture_spectrum.returns: Numpy float array, same length as axis, of the summed Gaussian components at each axis sample; non-negative when noise is added because the result is clipped at zero.

params.n: Number of depth samples in the synthetic well (default 600).
params.n_facies: Number of facies blocks, from 1 to 4, indexing the rows of LOG_SUITE_FACIES_PROPS (default 4).
params.rng: Random source: a numpy Generator, an integer seed, or None for the deterministic default_rng(0).

log_suite.returns: Tuple (logs, boundaries, facies_labels): a dict mapping the curve names GR, RD, RHOB, DT, NPHI to length-n numpy float arrays, an integer array of interior breakpoint indices, and a length-n integer array of the per-sample facies index.

params.n: Length of each generated curve in samples.
params.noise: Standard deviation of independent Gaussian noise added to each curve (default 0.0, noise-free).
params.rng: Random source used only when noise > 0: a numpy Generator, an integer seed, or None for the deterministic default_rng(0).
params.shift: Integer lag in samples between the two curves (other[i] = ref[i + shift] noise-free); may be negative.

shifted_pair.returns: Tuple (ref, other) of length-n numpy float arrays: a multi-harmonic reference curve and its copy lagged by shift samples, so a depth-alignment method should recover the shift.

params.max_grains: Safety cap on the number of spheres stamped before stopping (default 100000).
params.r_range: (low, high) inclusive range of integer sphere radii in voxels, drawn uniformly per grain.
params.rng: Random source: a numpy Generator, an integer seed, or None for the deterministic default_rng(0).
params.shape: (nx, ny, nz) voxel dimensions of the output grid.
params.target_phi: Target pore (void) fraction; sphere stamping stops once the pore fraction drops to this value.

sphere_pack_volume.returns: Numpy uint8 array of the given shape with 0 marking pore voxels and 1 marking grain voxels: a random sphere pack whose porosity is approximately target_phi.

params.cable_weight_per_length: Buoyant weight of the cable per unit length, in newtons per metre.
params.depth: Depth(s) z along the cable at which the tension is evaluated, in metres.
params.tool_weight: Weight of the tool hanging at the bottom of the cable, in newtons.
params.total_depth: Total cable length L (the tool depth), in metres.

cable_tension.returns: Cable tension profile T(z) = W_tool + w*(L - z) as a numpy float64 array; newtons with SI inputs. Greatest at surface and equal to tool_weight at the tool.

params.convention: Sign convention: "tally" (default) adds the corrections because the surface tally reads short; "payout" subtracts them because paid-out length overstates the tool depth; other values raise ValueError.
params.measured: Measured depth to be corrected (surface tally or paid-out cable length), in metres.
params.stretch: Elastic-stretch correction to apply, in metres; default 0.0.
params.thermal: Thermal-elongation correction to apply, in metres; default 0.0.

corrected_depth.returns: Corrected depth as a numpy float64 array in the units of measured: the measured depth plus (tally) or minus (payout) the sum of the stretch and thermal corrections.

params.buoyancy: Dimensionless buoyancy factor scaling the string's own-weight (quadratic) term; default 1.0.
params.ea: Lumped axial stiffness E*A of the string (Young's modulus times cross-section), in newtons with SI inputs.
params.end_load: Point load F carried at the bottom of the string, in newtons; default 0.0.
params.length: Suspended length L of the hanging string, in metres.
params.weight_per_length: Buoyant weight per unit length w of the string, in newtons per metre.

distributed_stretch.returns: Total hanging-string stretch dL = (F*L + 0.5*b*w*L**2)/(E*A), combining the end-load and self-weight contributions, as a numpy float64 array; metres with SI inputs.

params.E: Young's modulus of the member material, in pascals; defaults to the steel value E_STEEL = 2.0e11 Pa.
params.area: Metal cross-sectional area of the member, in square metres.
params.force: Axial load applied to the cable or pipe, in newtons.
params.length: Suspended length of the member subject to the load, in metres.

elastic_stretch.returns: Point-load Hookean stretch dL = F*L/(E*A) as a numpy float64 array; metres with SI inputs.

params.alpha: Linear thermal-expansion coefficient, in 1/K; defaults to the steel value ALPHA_STEEL = 1.2e-5.
params.dT: Temperature rise above the calibration temperature, in kelvin (or degrees celsius difference).
params.length: Length of the member being heated, in metres.

thermal_elongation.returns: Thermal elongation dL = alpha*L*dT as a numpy float64 array; metres with SI inputs.

params.depth: Depth coordinates of the samples, in any consistent depth unit.
params.shift: Depth shift in the same units as depth; either a scalar bulk shift or a per-sample array.
params.values: Curve values sampled on the depth grid.

apply_depth_shift.returns: Float64 array of the curve resampled by linear interpolation from depth + shift back onto the original depth grid, with values beyond the shifted range clamped to the first/last input values.

params.fill: Value written into the samples vacated at the exposed end; default NaN.
params.lag: Integer shift in samples; a positive lag moves samples to greater depth (higher index).

apply_integer_shift.returns: Float64 array of the same length as the input with all samples moved by lag without wrapping; the vacated end is filled with fill.

params.n_segments: Number of piecewise segments; the n_segments - 1 interior boundaries are optimised greedily; default 10.
params.ref: Reference curve defining the output grid and segment layout.
params.slack: Maximum boundary displacement in samples (each boundary is tried within +/-slack); default 8.
params.target: Target curve whose interior segment boundaries are adjusted.

cow.returns: Tuple of two float64 arrays (aligned, warp): aligned is the target piecewise-linearly remapped onto the reference grid (same length as ref); warp is the fractional target-index coordinate assigned to each reference sample.

params.band: Sakoe-Chiba band half-width in samples restricting how far the path may stray from the diagonal; None (default) allows the full matrix.
params.cost: Local cost function: 'sqdiff' (default) uses the squared sample difference, 'absdiff' the absolute difference.
params.ref: Reference 1-D curve onto which the target is warped.
params.root: If True, the returned distance is the square root of the accumulated total cost; default False.
params.target: Target 1-D curve to be aligned to the reference.

dtw.returns: DtwResult named tuple: distance (float total accumulated cost, square-rooted if root=True), path (list of 0-based (ref_index, target_index) pairs from backtracking), and cost (the (n+1, m+1) accumulated cost matrix as a float64 array).

params.max_lag: Clip limit: each window's estimated lag is clipped to [-max_lag, max_lag] samples; default 20.
params.step: Stride between successive window start positions, in samples; default 30.
params.target: Target 1-D curve whose local lags relative to the reference are estimated.
params.window: Sliding-window length in samples over which each local lag is estimated; default 60.

local_shifts.returns: Float64 array with one entry per window position: the integer lag (in samples, stored as float) that maximises the mean-removed full cross-correlation of the target window against the reference window.

params.depth_ref: Depth coordinates of the reference samples, in any consistent depth unit.
params.depth_target: Depth coordinates of the target samples, in the same unit as depth_ref.
params.path: DTW warping path as a list of (ref_index, target_index) pairs.

path_depth_shifts.returns: Tuple of two float64 arrays, one entry per path pair: the reference depths depth_ref[i] and the per-depth shifts depth_target[j] - depth_ref[i], in the supplied depth units.

params.band: Sakoe-Chiba band half-width in samples; None (default) allows the full matrix.
params.lam: Weight of the off-diagonal penalty lam*|i/n - j/m| that discourages excessive warping; default 0.5.
params.ref: Reference 1-D curve onto which the target is warped.
params.target: Target 1-D curve to be aligned to the reference.
params.tau: Weight on the Keogh-Pazzani derivative (trend) absolute-difference term in the local cost; default 4.0.

rddtw.returns: DtwResult named tuple: distance (float total accumulated cost), path (list of 0-based (ref_index, target_index) pairs), and cost (the accumulated cost matrix as a float64 array).

params.n_ref: Length of the reference grid, i.e. the number of output samples.
params.path: DTW warping path as a list of (ref_index, target_index) pairs, e.g. DtwResult.path.
params.reduce: How multiply-matched target samples are combined per reference index; only 'mean' is accepted.
params.target: Target curve whose samples are collapsed onto the reference grid.

warp_to_reference.returns: Float64 array of length n_ref: the target curve aligned to the reference grid, with reference indices absent from the path filled from the nearest populated index.

params.edge: Edge handling: 'trim' (default) correlates only the overlapping samples at each lag; 'wrap' uses a circular np.roll.
params.max_lag: Maximum integer lag magnitude scanned, in samples; lags run over [-max_lag, max_lag]; default 50.
params.return_curve: If True, the full correlation-versus-lag curve is included in the result; otherwise the curve field is None.
params.target: Target 1-D curve to be shifted onto the reference.

xcorr_shift.returns: ShiftResult named tuple: lag (int best lag in samples; a positive lag moves the target to greater depth/index), corr (float Pearson correlation at that lag), and curve (float64 correlation-versus-lag array if return_curve=True, else None).

params.a: Curve sampled on depth_a (e.g. core data) that is slid against b.
params.b: Log curve on depth_b; a single 1-D curve or an (n, k) multi-curve array whose columns are each tried.
params.depth_a: Depth coordinates of curve a, in physical depth units (e.g. m or ft).
params.depth_b: Depth coordinates of the log curve(s) b, in the same depth units.
params.max_shift: Maximum shift magnitude scanned, in the same depth units as the coordinates; default 2.0.
params.step: Shift increment of the scan, in depth units; default 0.125.
params.use_abs_corr: If True, the absolute value of the Pearson correlation is maximised (allows anticorrelated curves); default False.

xcorr_shift_depth.returns: Tuple of two floats (best_shift, best_corr): the depth shift (added to depth_a) that maximises the correlation, in depth units, and the signed Pearson correlation achieved at that shift.

anisotropy_coefficient.returns: Dimensionless resistivity anisotropy coefficient lambda = sqrt(Rv/Rh) (the canonical definition), as a float64 numpy array.

params.r_invasion: Invasion radius in meters, setting the geometric factor of the invaded zone.
params.sigma_t: Conductivity of the virgin (uninvaded) formation, in S/m.
params.sigma_xo: Conductivity of the invaded (flushed) zone, in S/m.

apparent_conductivity_two_zone.returns: Doll two-zone apparent conductivity sigma_a = G_xo*sigma_xo + (1 - G_xo)*sigma_t in S/m, where G_xo is the radial geometric factor at the invasion radius, as a float64 numpy array.

params.dip_deg: Relative dip angle theta between the tool axis and the bedding normal, in degrees.

apparent_resistivity_dip.returns: Apparent resistivity Rh*sqrt(cos(theta)**2 + (Rv/Rh)*sin(theta)**2) in ohm.m seen by an induction tool at relative dip, as a float64 numpy array.

params.rho: Formation resistivity in ohm.m.
params.spacing_m: Receiver spacing L in meters over which the amplitude decays.

attenuation_db.returns: Induction-limit amplitude attenuation 8.686*L/delta in decibels (nepers converted to dB), as a float64 numpy array.

params.v_far: Complex voltage measured at the far receiver.
params.v_near: Complex voltage measured at the near receiver.

attenuation_phase_from_voltages.returns: Tuple of two float64 arrays: the attenuation 20*log10(abs(v_near/v_far)) in dB, and the phase shift of the voltage ratio in degrees.

params.eps_components: Permittivities of the N phases (real or complex), indexed along the leading axis 0.
params.fractions: Volume fractions of the N phases, indexed along the leading axis 0.
params.iterations: Number of Newton iterations used to solve the implicit effective-medium equation; default 60.

bruggeman_symmetric.returns: Symmetric Bruggeman (spherical-grain) effective permittivity solving sum_i f_i*(eps_i - eps_eff)/(eps_i + 2*eps_eff) = 0, as a numpy array; float64 for real inputs, complex128 for complex.

params.eps_hc: Real relative permittivity of the hydrocarbon phase (required keyword).
params.eps_matrix: Real relative permittivity of the rock matrix (required keyword).
params.eps_w: Real relative permittivity of the pore water (required keyword).
params.phi: Total porosity as a volume fraction.

bvw_from_permittivity.returns: Bulk volume water phi*Sw (fraction of bulk rock volume) from the CRIM inversion, unclipped, as a float64 numpy array.

params.alpha: Cole-Cole distribution (broadening) exponent, dimensionless; 0 (the default) recovers the Debye model.
params.freq_hz: Frequency (or frequency array) in Hz at which the model is evaluated.

cole_cole.returns: Complex Cole-Cole permittivity eps_inf + (eps_s - eps_inf)/(1 + (j*omega*tau)**(1-alpha)), as a complex128 numpy array.

params.c: Frequency exponent of the Cole-Cole dispersion, dimensionless.
params.freq_hz: Frequency (or frequency array) in Hz at which the model is evaluated.

cole_cole_resistivity.returns: Complex resistivity rho(omega) = rho0*(1 - m*(1 - 1/(1 + (j*omega*tau)**c))) in ohm.m, the Pelton induced-polarization form, as a complex128 numpy array.

params.eps_imag: Dielectric loss eps'' already expressed in relative-permittivity units, added to the imaginary part; default 0.
params.eps_real: Real part eps' of the relative permittivity (dimensionless).
params.freq_hz: Frequency in Hz used to form omega = 2*pi*f; required only when sigma is nonzero, otherwise may be None.
params.sigma: Electrical conductivity in S/m whose ohmic loss sigma/(omega*eps0) is added to the imaginary part; default 0.

complex_permittivity.returns: Complex relative permittivity eps* = eps' - j*(eps'' + sigma/(omega*eps0)) in the -j engineering convention, as a complex128 numpy array (positive conductivity gives a negative imaginary part).

params.eps_r: Real relative permittivity of the medium.

complex_wavenumber.returns: Tuple of two float64 arrays (k_r, k_i): the phase constant beta and attenuation constant alpha of the exact lossy-medium wavenumber k = beta - j*alpha, both in 1/m (rad/m and Np/m).

params.alpha: Power-law mixing exponent; 0.5 (default) is the classic CRIM square-root mixing.
params.eps_hc: Real relative permittivity of the hydrocarbon phase; default 2.2.
params.eps_matrix: Real relative permittivity of the rock matrix; default 5.0.
params.eps_w: Real relative permittivity of the pore water; default 78.0.
params.phi: Total porosity as a volume fraction (v/v).
params.sw: Water saturation of the pore space, fraction; default 1.0 (fully water-filled).

crim.returns: Effective rock permittivity from the three-component (water/hydrocarbon/matrix) power-law forward model, dimensionless, as a float64 numpy array.

params.freq_hz: Frequency (or frequency array) in Hz at which the model is evaluated.

debye.returns: Complex permittivity of the single-pole Debye model, eps_inf + (eps_s - eps_inf)/(1 + j*omega*tau), as a complex128 numpy array over the input frequencies.

params.aspect: Spheroid aspect ratio c/a (polar over equatorial semi-axis): 1 is a sphere, less than 1 oblate (disk), greater than 1 prolate (needle).

depolarization_spheroid.returns: Tuple of three float64 arrays (Lx, Ly, Lz): the Osborn depolarization factors, dimensionless and summing to 1, with Lz along the polar symmetry axis and Lx = Ly = (1 - Lz)/2.

params.r: Radial distance from the tool axis to which the geometric factor is integrated, in meters (same units as spacing_m).

doll_radial_geometric_factor.returns: Dimensionless integrated Doll radial geometric factor G = r**2/(r**2 + (L/2)**2), between 0 and 1, as a float64 numpy array.

params.eps_host: Permittivity of the continuous host phase; real or complex.
params.eps_incl: Permittivity of the inclusion phase; real or complex.
params.f_incl: Volume fraction of the inclusions (0 to 1).
params.iterations: Number of Newton iterations used to solve the implicit Hanai equation; default 80.

hanai_bruggeman.returns: Asymmetric (Hanai) Bruggeman effective permittivity solving ((eps_eff - eps_i)/(eps_h - eps_i))*(eps_h/eps_eff)**(1/3) = 1 - f, as a numpy array; float64 for real inputs, complex128 for complex.

params.alpha: Symmetric broadening exponent applied to (j*omega*tau); default 1.0.
params.beta: Asymmetric broadening exponent applied to the whole denominator; alpha = beta = 1 recovers the Debye model.
params.freq_hz: Frequency (or frequency array) in Hz at which the model is evaluated.

havriliak_negami.returns: Complex Havriliak-Negami permittivity eps_inf + (eps_s - eps_inf)/(1 + (j*omega*tau)**alpha)**beta, as a complex128 numpy array.

imag_permittivity_from_sigma.returns: Loss (imaginary) relative permittivity eps'' = sigma/(2*pi*f*eps0), dimensionless, as a float64 numpy array.

params.eps_r: Real relative permittivity of the medium (dimensionless).

impedivity.returns: Complex impedivity 1/(j*omega*eps0*eps_r + sigma), the inverse of the complex admittivity, in ohm.m, as a complex128 numpy array.

params.rho: Formation resistivity in ohm.m.

induction_number.returns: Dimensionless induction number L/delta (spacing over skin depth), the near-field versus far-field discriminator, as a float64 numpy array.

params.eps_star: Complex permittivity in the -j loss convention (e.g. the output of complex_permittivity).

loss_tangent.returns: Loss tangent tan(delta) = -Im(eps*)/Re(eps*), dimensionless, as a float64 numpy array; positive for lossy media in the -j convention.

params.depol: Depolarization factor N of the inclusion shape; default 1/3 (spheres).
params.eps_host: Permittivity of the continuous host (background) phase; real or complex.
params.eps_incl: Permittivity of the dispersed inclusions; real or complex.
params.f_incl: Volume fraction of the inclusions (0 to 1).

maxwell_garnett.returns: Maxwell-Garnett effective permittivity eps_h*(1 + f*ds/(eps_h + N*(1-f)*ds)) with ds = eps_incl - eps_host, as a numpy array; float64 for real inputs, complex128 for complex; the same formula also mixes conductivities.

params.alpha: Lichtenecker-Rother power-law exponent; 0.5 (the default) is the CRIM square-root (time-average) mixing law.
params.eps_components: Component permittivities (real, or complex to carry loss), indexed along the leading axis 0.
params.fractions: Volume fractions of the mixture components, indexed along the leading axis 0 and broadcastable with eps_components.

mix_power_law.returns: Effective mixture permittivity (sum_i f_i*eps_i**alpha)**(1/alpha), summed over axis 0, as a numpy array; float64 for real inputs, complex128 when the components are complex.

params.rho: Formation resistivity in ohm.m.
params.spacing_m: Receiver spacing L in meters over which the phase shift accumulates.

phase_shift_deg.returns: Induction-limit phase shift over the spacing, degrees(L/delta), in degrees, as a float64 numpy array.

params.phase_deg: Measured phase shift between receivers, in degrees.

resistivity_from_phase.returns: Apparent resistivity rho = L**2*omega*mu0/(2*phase_rad**2) in ohm.m, the induction-limit inverse of phase_shift_deg, as a float64 numpy array.

params.k_i: Attenuation constant alpha of the wavenumber, in 1/m.
params.k_r: Phase constant beta of the wavenumber, in 1/m.

sigma_eps_from_wavenumber.returns: Tuple of two float64 arrays (sigma, eps_r): the conductivity in S/m and the real relative permittivity recovered in closed form from k**2 = omega**2*mu*eps*.

sigma_from_imag_permittivity.returns: Equivalent electrical conductivity sigma = eps''*2*pi*f*eps0 in S/m, as a float64 numpy array.

params.mu_r: Relative magnetic permeability of the medium; default 1.0.
params.rho: Formation resistivity in ohm.m.

skin_depth.returns: Electromagnetic skin depth delta = sqrt(2*rho/(omega*mu0*mu_r)) in meters, as a float64 numpy array.

params.clip: If True (default) the computed saturation is clipped to the physical interval [0, 1]; False returns the raw value.
params.eps_hc: Real relative permittivity of the hydrocarbon phase (required keyword).
params.eps_matrix: Real relative permittivity of the rock matrix (required keyword).
params.eps_w: Real relative permittivity of the pore water (required keyword).
params.phi: Total porosity as a volume fraction.

sw_from_permittivity.returns: Water saturation Sw (fraction of pore volume) from the closed-form CRIM inversion, as a float64 numpy array; bounded to [0, 1] when clip=True.

params.clip: If True (default) the computed porosity is clipped to [0, 1]; False returns the raw value.
params.eps_matrix: Real relative permittivity of the rock matrix (required keyword).
params.eps_w: Real relative permittivity of the pore water (required keyword).

water_filled_porosity.returns: Water-filled porosity phi_w (fraction of bulk volume) from the two-component water/matrix CRIM inversion, as a float64 numpy array; bounded to [0, 1] when clip=True.

params.eps_dl: Extra dielectric-loss (double-layer) term added to the imaginary part, in relative-permittivity units; default 15.0.
params.eps_real: Real relative permittivity of the water, dimensionless; default 55.0.
params.freq_hz: Frequency in Hz (GHz-band dielectric logging frequencies).
params.rw: Brine (formation-water) resistivity in ohm.m; the brine conductivity is taken as sigma_w = 1/rw.

water_permittivity.returns: Complex relative permittivity of the brine, eps_real - j*(eps_dl + sigma_w/(omega*eps0)), in the -j convention, as a complex128 numpy array.

params.D: Dispersion coefficient (SI: m2/s), applied with a central-difference scheme.

params.length: Length of the one-dimensional domain (SI: m).
params.n_cells: Number of finite-difference grid cells discretizing the domain.
params.n_steps: Optional explicit number of time steps; None (default) uses the CFL/diffusion-stable step dt = 0.4*min(dx/v, dx**2/(2*D)).
params.t_total: Total simulated time at which the profile is returned (SI: s).

advect_disperse_1d.returns: Tuple (x, C) of numpy float64 arrays, each of length n_cells: the cell-centre coordinates on [0, length] and the concentration profile at t_total, clipped to the range [0, c_in].

params.fzi_values: Array of per-sample Flow Zone Indicator values; values are floored at 1e-6 before taking log10.
params.n_units: Number of hydraulic flow units to define via log-spaced FZI thresholds; default 4.

classify_hfu.returns: Tuple (labels, thresholds): an integer numpy array of unit labels in [0, n_units-1] for each sample, and a numpy float64 array of the n_units+1 FZI threshold values, log-spaced between the minimum and maximum FZI.

params.area: Cross-sectional flow area of the sample (SI: m2).
params.length: Length of the sample along the flow direction (SI: m).
params.p_ref: Reference absolute pressure at which the rate q is measured, e.g. outlet or atmospheric (SI: Pa).
params.q: Volumetric gas flow rate measured at the reference pressure p_ref (SI: m3/s).

darcy_gas_permeability.returns: Compressible gas permeability from the pressure-squared form k = 2*q*mu*L*P_ref/(A*(P_up**2 - P_down**2)), a numpy float64 array; m2 with SI inputs.

params.area: Cross-sectional flow area of the sample (SI: m2).
params.dp: Pressure drop across the sample (SI: Pa).
params.length: Length of the sample along the flow direction (SI: m).
params.mu: Dynamic viscosity of the flowing liquid (SI: Pa.s).
params.q: Volumetric flow rate of the liquid through the core plug, in any consistent unit set (SI: m3/s).

darcy_permeability.returns: Darcy permeability k = q*mu*L/(A*dP) as a numpy float64 array; m2 with SI inputs, or whatever the chosen consistent unit set implies.

params.area: Cross-sectional flow area of the sample (SI: m2).
params.k: Absolute permeability of the sample (SI: m2).

params.length: Length of the sample along the flow direction (SI: m).
params.mu: Dynamic viscosity of the flowing phase (SI: Pa.s).
params.q: Volumetric flow rate through the sample (SI: m3/s).

darcy_pressure_drop.returns: Pressure drop dP = q*mu*L/(k*kr*A) across the sample as a numpy float64 array; Pa with SI inputs.

params.area: Cross-sectional flow area of the sample (SI: m2).
params.dp: Pressure drop across the sample (SI: Pa).
params.length: Length of the sample along the flow direction (SI: m).
params.mu: Dynamic viscosity of the flowing fluid (SI: Pa.s).

darcy_rate.returns: Volumetric Darcy flow rate q = k*A*dP/(mu*L) as a numpy float64 array; m3/s with SI inputs. The inverse of darcy_permeability.

params.geometry_factor: Dimensionless factor f in L = sqrt(f*D*t); 1.0 (default) in most articles, 2 for the sqrt(2*D*t) displacement form.

diffusion_length.returns: Characteristic diffusion length L = sqrt(f*D*t) as a numpy float64 array; metres with SI inputs.

params.L: Diffusion distance or characteristic length (SI: m).
params.geometry_factor: Dimensionless factor f in t = L**2/(f*D); default 1.0, 2 for the displacement form, pi**2 for a sealed-column mixing time.

diffusion_time.returns: Diffusion or equilibration time t = L**2/(f*D) as a numpy float64 array; seconds with SI inputs. The inverse of diffusion_length.

params.half_length: Half-length L of the slab into which the species diffuses (SI: m).
params.t: Elapsed time(s) since exposure began (SI: s).

early_time_uptake.returns: Dimensionless early-time fractional uptake Mt/Minf = (2/L)*sqrt(D*t/pi) as a numpy float64 array, valid up to roughly 0.6.

params.c0: Fixed boundary concentration held at x = 0; sets the units of the result.
params.t: Elapsed time since the boundary concentration was imposed (SI: s).
params.x: Distance(s) from the boundary into the semi-infinite medium (SI: m).

erfc_profile.returns: Semi-infinite diffusion concentration profile C(x,t) = C0*erfc(x/(2*sqrt(D*t))) as a numpy float64 array, in the units of c0 (far-field value 0). Uses scipy.special.erfc, imported lazily.

params.dc: Concentration difference across the distance dx, in the caller's concentration units.
params.dx: Distance over which the concentration difference applies (SI: m).

fick_flux.returns: Steady diffusive flux J = -D*dc/dx from Fick's first law, as a numpy float64 array in the concentration unit times m/s with SI inputs; the sign opposes the concentration gradient.

params.k_app: Array of measured apparent gas permeabilities, one per mean pressure.
params.p_mean: Array of mean gas pressures at which the apparent permeabilities were measured.

fit_klinkenberg.returns: Tuple (k_inf, b) of Python floats from a straight-line fit of k_app versus 1/P: the intercept is the intrinsic permeability k_inf (units of k_app) and b = slope/intercept is the slip factor (units of p_mean).

params.k_md: Array of permeabilities in millidarcy paired with phi.
params.phi: Array of porosity fractions of the calibration samples.

fit_poroperm.returns: Tuple (a, b) of Python floats, the intercept and slope of the least-squares fit of log10(k) on log10(phi), directly usable in poroperm_powerlaw.

params.k: Array of measured permeabilities, one per stress point.
params.ncs: Array of net confining stresses at which permeability was measured.

fit_stress_permeability.returns: Tuple (k0, gamma) of Python floats from a line fit in (NCS, ln k) space: k0 = exp(intercept) in the units of k, gamma = -slope in 1/stress units.

params.cuts: Three ascending Knudsen-number cutoffs separating continuum/slip, slip/transition, and transition/free-molecular regimes; default (1e-3, 0.1, 10.0).
params.kn: Knudsen number of the gas flow, a scalar float.

flow_regime.returns: Regime name as a string: "continuum" for Kn below cuts[0], "slip" below cuts[1], "transition" below cuts[2], otherwise "free-molecular".

params.c: RQI unit-conversion constant passed through to rqi; default 0.0314.
params.phi: Porosity as a fraction.

fzi.returns: Amaefule Flow Zone Indicator FZI = RQI/phi_z in micrometres, as a numpy float64 array.

params.fzi_val: Flow Zone Indicator value(s) in micrometres.
params.phi: Porosity as a fraction.

params.b: Klinkenberg gas-slip factor, in the same pressure units as p_mean.
params.c2: Optional second-order slip coefficient (units of pressure squared); default 0.0 gives the first-order Klinkenberg law.
params.k_inf: Intrinsic (infinite-pressure, liquid-equivalent) permeability; sets the units of the result.
params.p_mean: Mean gas pressure of the measurement, sharing units with b.

params.p_mean: Mean gas pressure of the measurement, sharing units with b.

params.t1: Temperature at which r1 is known, in the unit selected by unit.
