params.unit: Temperature unit, 'F' (Arps constant 6.77) or 'C' (constant 21.5); any other value raises ValueError.

arps_correct.returns: Resistivity at t2, R2 = R1*(T1+c)/(T2+c), in ohm-m as a numpy float64 array.

params.nacl_ppm: NaCl salinity in ppm, converted internally to a weight fraction S = ppm/1e6.
params.press_mpa: Pressure in MPa (default 0.1, atmospheric); contributes a small linear term of 0.045*(P-0.1) g/cm3 equivalent.

brine_density_bw92.returns: Simplified Batzle-Wang (1992) brine density in kg/m3 as a numpy float64 array: pure-water density plus an NaCl salinity term plus a small linear pressure term.

nacl_meq_per_liter.returns: NaCl concentration in milliequivalents per liter, ppm/58.44 (assumes a monovalent salt and brine density near 1 g/cm3), as a numpy float64 array.

rw75_from_salinity.returns: Bateman-Konen water resistivity at 75 degF, R75 = 0.0123 + 3647.5/C**0.955, in ohm-m as a numpy float64 array.

params.temp: Formation temperature at which the resistivity is wanted, in the unit selected by unit.
params.unit: Temperature unit of temp, 'F' (default) or 'C'; Celsius input is converted to Fahrenheit before the Arps correction from the 75 degF reference.

rw_from_salinity.returns: Water resistivity at the given temperature in ohm-m as a numpy float64 array (Bateman-Konen R75 followed by the Arps temperature correction).

params.rw: Water resistivity at temperature temp, in ohm-m.
params.temp: Temperature at which rw is measured, in the unit selected by unit.
params.unit: Temperature unit of temp, 'F' (default) or 'C'; Celsius input is converted to Fahrenheit first.

salinity_from_rw.returns: NaCl-equivalent salinity in ppm as a numpy float64 array; inverse of rw_from_salinity (Arps-corrects rw to 75 degF, then inverts Bateman-Konen).

params.rw75_ohmm: Water resistivity at 75 degF in ohm-m.

salinity_from_rw75.returns: NaCl-equivalent salinity in ppm as a numpy float64 array; exact algebraic inverse of rw75_from_salinity.

params.nacl_ppm: NaCl-equivalent salinity in ppm, converted internally to a weight fraction w = ppm/1e6.
params.temp_c: Brine temperature in degrees Celsius (default 24.0), entering the linear correction factor (1 - 8e-4*(T - 75)).

sigma_w_from_salinity.returns: Thermal-neutron capture cross-section of the brine, Sigma_w = (22 + 750*w)*(1 - 8e-4*(T-75)), in capture units (c.u.) as a numpy float64 array; 22 c.u. is the fresh-water baseline.

params.eta0: Initial contamination fraction at zero pumped volume.
params.exponent: Power-law decay exponent; the default 5/12 is the wireline-cleanup value, 2/3 applies to radial flow.
params.v: Cumulative pumped volume, in the same (arbitrary) unit as v_star.
params.v_star: Characteristic cleanup volume setting the scale of the power-law decline, same unit as v.

cleanup_powerlaw.returns: Contamination fraction after pumping, eta(V) = eta0*(1 + V/V_star)**(-exponent), dimensionless, as a numpy float64 array.

params.p: Measured property of the sampled (mixed) fluid, same unit as the endpoints.
params.p_f: Property value of the pure filtrate endpoint.
params.p_v: Property value of the virgin formation fluid endpoint.

contamination_fraction.returns: Contamination fraction eta = (P - P_v)/(P_f - P_v), dimensionless, as a numpy float64 array; inverse of the linear mixing rule.

params.eta: Filtrate (contamination) volume fraction of the mixture, 0 to 1.
params.p_f: Property value of the pure mud filtrate, same unit as p_v.
params.p_v: Property value of the virgin (uncontaminated) formation fluid, any unit.

mix_linear.returns: Linearly mixed property P = (1-eta)*P_virgin + eta*P_filtrate, in the same unit as the endpoints, as a numpy float64 array.

params.eta0: Initial contamination fraction at zero pumped volume.
params.eta_t: Target contamination fraction to be reached.
params.exponent: Power-law decay exponent of the cleanup model; default 5/12 (wireline), 2/3 for radial.
params.v_star: Characteristic cleanup volume of the power-law model; sets the unit of the returned volume.

volume_to_target.returns: Pumped volume required to reach the target contamination, V = V_star*((eta0/eta_t)**(1/exponent) - 1), in the unit of v_star, as a numpy float64 array.

params.v_bulk: Optional bulk volume of the core sample; when omitted the returned porosity is NaN.
params.v_hc: Volume of hydrocarbon recovered, same unit as v_water.
params.v_pore: Optional independently measured pore volume; when omitted the pore volume defaults to v_water + v_hc.
params.v_water: Volume of water recovered from the core by Dean-Stark extraction (any consistent volume unit).

dean_stark.returns: Tuple of three numpy float64 arrays (phi, Sw, S_hc): porosity pore/v_bulk (NaN where v_bulk is not given), water saturation v_water/pore, and hydrocarbon saturation v_hc/pore, all as fractions.

params.s1_mg_g: Rock-Eval S1 free-hydrocarbon yield in mg HC per g rock.
params.toc_wt_pct: Total organic carbon in weight percent.

osi.returns: Jarvie oil saturation index OSI = 100*S1/TOC in mg HC per g TOC, as a numpy float64 array; values above ~100 indicate producible oil.

params.axis: Array axis along which the suite is summed and normalized (default -1, the last axis).
params.oxides: Oxide (or elemental) weight fractions of the suite, arranged with the components along axis.

oxide_closure.returns: Tuple (closed, factor) of numpy float64 arrays: the suite rescaled to sum to one along axis, and the closure factor 1/sum(oxides) with the summed axis removed.

params.dpdz: Pressure gradient value(s), expressed in the unit named by p_unit; no clipping is applied, so a negative gradient yields a negative density.
params.p_unit: Gradient unit switch: 'Pa' (Pa/m, default), 'psi/ft', or 'bar/m'; any other value raises ValueError.

density_from_gradient.returns: Fluid density rho = gradient_SI / g (standard gravity 9.80665 m/s2) in kg/m3 as a numpy float64 array.

params.p: Formation pressures at each depth, in Pa (SI module convention).

fit_pressure_gradient.returns: Tuple of two scalar floats (slope, intercept): the least-squares pressure gradient dP/dz in pressure unit per m, and the zero-depth intercept P0 in the pressure unit.

params.depth_a: Depths of the pressure points in the first fluid column, in m.
params.depth_b: Depths of the pressure points in the second fluid column, in m.
params.p_a: Pressures of the first fluid column at depth_a, in Pa.
params.p_b: Pressures of the second fluid column at depth_b, in Pa.

fluid_contact.returns: Scalar float contact depth in m where the two least-squares pressure-depth lines intersect (e.g. a GOC or OWC).

params.alphas: Per-component extraction-efficiency coefficients; each component is divided by its alpha.
params.comps: Raw measured component readings to be corrected (any consistent unit).

apply_eec.returns: Extraction-efficiency-corrected composition comps/alphas as a numpy float64 array, in the same unit as comps.

params.c1: Methane (C1) reading, any unit consistent with the other components.

balance_ratio.returns: Haworth balance ratio Bh = (C1+...+C5)/(C3+C4+C5), dimensionless, as a numpy float64 array; inf where the C3+C4+C5 denominator is zero.

params.c1: Methane (C1) reading, any unit consistent with the other components.

bernard_ratio.returns: Bernard ratio C1/(C2+C3), a biogenic-versus-thermogenic gas indicator, as a numpy float64 array; inf where the C2+C3 denominator is zero.

params.c3: Propane (C3) reading, any unit consistent with the other components.

character_ratio.returns: Haworth character ratio Ch = (C4+C5)/C3, dimensionless, as a numpy float64 array; 0 where C3 is zero.

params.thresholds: Ascending GOR boundaries separating the five fluid classes; default (180, 360, 640, 5000, 15000) Sm3/Sm3.

classify_fluid_gor.returns: Fluid-type label string: 'black oil', 'volatile oil', 'gas condensate', 'wet gas', or 'dry gas', chosen as the first class whose upper threshold exceeds the GOR ('dry gas' above all thresholds).

params.bh: Haworth balance ratio (scalar); in the 17.5-40 percent wetness band, Bh > 100 selects 'gas condensate' over 'oil'.
params.wh_pct: Haworth wetness ratio in percent (0-100), scalar; the thresholds assume the percent convention.

classify_fluid_wetness.returns: Fluid-type label string: 'dry gas' (Wh < 0.5), 'gas' (Wh < 17.5), 'gas condensate' or 'oil' (17.5 <= Wh < 40, split on Bh > 100), or 'residual oil' (Wh >= 40).

params.axis: Array axis along which the components are summed and normalized (default -1).
params.comps: Component readings of the composition (any consistent unit), arranged with the components along axis.

normalize_composition.returns: Composition rescaled to sum to one along axis as a numpy float64 array; groups whose sum is zero return zeros instead of dividing by zero.

params.c1: Methane (C1) reading, any unit consistent with the other components.

pixler_ratios.returns: Dict with keys 'C1/C2', 'C1/C3', 'C1/C4', and 'C1/C5', each a numpy float64 array of the corresponding dimensionless Pixler ratio; inf where a denominator is zero.

params.c1: Methane (C1) reading, any unit consistent with the other components.
params.percent: If True (default) the ratio is returned as a percent (0-100); if False, as a fraction (0-1).

wetness_ratio.returns: Haworth wetness ratio Wh = (C2+C3+C4+C5)/(C1+...+C5) as a numpy float64 array, in percent by default or as a fraction with percent=False.

params.m_kg_mol: Gas molar mass in kg/mol; defaults to methane (0.01604 kg/mol).
params.z: Gas compressibility factor (dimensionless); defaults to 1.0 (ideal gas).

gas_density.returns: Real-gas density rho = P*M/(z*R*T) in kg/m3 as a numpy float64 array.

gas_gravity.returns: Gas specific gravity MW_gas/MW_air (air = 0.028964 kg/mol), dimensionless, as a numpy float64 array.

params.axis: Array axis along which the mole-fraction-weighted sum is taken (default -1).
params.mw: Component molecular weights, in kg/mol under the module's SI convention (any consistent unit carries through).
params.y: Component mole fractions of the mixture, arranged along axis.

mixture_mw.returns: Mixture molecular weight sum(y_i*MW_i) in the unit of mw, as a numpy float64 array with the summed axis removed.

params.m_kg_mol: Gas molar mass in kg/mol; defaults to methane (0.01604 kg/mol).
params.rho: Gas density in kg/m3.

pressure_from_gas_density.returns: Pressure P = rho*z*R*T/M in Pa as a numpy float64 array; inverse of gas_density.

params.p: Absolute pressure, in the same unit as ppc (Pa in the module's strict-SI convention).
params.ppc: Pseudocritical pressure of the gas mixture, same unit as p.
params.t: Absolute temperature, in the same unit as tpc (K).
params.tpc: Pseudocritical temperature of the gas mixture, same unit as t.

pseudo_reduced.returns: Tuple of two numpy float64 arrays (Ppr, Tpr): the dimensionless pseudo-reduced pressure P/Ppc and pseudo-reduced temperature T/Tpc.

params.k: Equilibrium K-values of the components (e.g. from wilson_k).
params.tol: Convergence tolerance on the absolute value of the Rachford-Rice objective (default 1e-12).
params.z: Overall (feed) mole fractions of the components.

rachford_rice.returns: Scalar float vapor mole fraction beta in [0, 1] solving sum(z*(k-1)/(1+beta*(k-1))) = 0 by bisection; when the objective does not change sign over [0, 1], the bracket endpoint with the smaller residual (0.0 or 1.0) is returned.

wilson_k.returns: Wilson (1969) equilibrium K-value estimate (Pc/P)*exp(5.373*(1+omega)*(1 - Tc/T)), dimensionless, as a numpy float64 array.

z_beggs_brill.returns: Beggs-Brill (1973) gas compressibility factor z (dimensionless) as a numpy float64 array.

params.omega: Acentric factor of the component (dimensionless), used in the PR alpha function.
params.phase: Root selector: 'vapor' (default) returns the largest positive real root of the PR cubic, 'liquid' the smallest positive real root; other values raise ValueError.

z_peng_robinson.returns: Scalar float Peng-Robinson compressibility factor z for the selected phase; raises ValueError when the cubic has no positive real root.

params.m_ch4: Dissolved methane molality in mol/kg (default 0.0); accounts for methane competition via the factor 1/(1 + 0.6*m_CH4).
params.m_nacl: NaCl molality of the brine in mol/kg (default 0.0, fresh water); reduces solubility via the Setschenow factor.

co2_solubility_brine.returns: CO2 solubility in brine, P/(H*gamma) reduced by the methane-competition factor, in mol/kg as a numpy float64 array.

henry_constant_co2.returns: Duan-Sun-style CO2 Henry constant H = 10*exp(-9.4234 + 4.0087e-2*T - 4.5e-5*T**2) in MPa per unit molality, as a numpy float64 array.

params.a: Fitted constant (intercept) of the Krichevsky-Kasarnovsky correlation.
params.b: Fitted coefficient multiplying ln(P) in the correlation.
params.dh_j_mol: Enthalpy of solution dH in J/mol, entering as -dH/(R*T).

henry_solubility_ln.returns: Natural logarithm of the dissolved-gas mole fraction in oil, ln(x) = a + b*ln(P) - dH/(R*T), as a numpy float64 array (exponentiate to get the mole fraction).

params.ks25: Setschenow salting-out coefficient at 298 K (25 degC); default 0.11.
params.m_nacl: NaCl molality of the brine in mol/kg.
params.t_k: Temperature in K; the Setschenow coefficient is adjusted linearly as ks = ks25 + 1e-4*(T - 298).

setschenow_factor.returns: Setschenow salting-out activity factor gamma = exp(2*ks*m), dimensionless, as a numpy float64 array; equals 1 for fresh water and grows with salinity.

params.rho: Bulk density of the medium, in kg/m3 when rho_unit is 'kg/m3' or in g/cm3 when rho_unit is 'g/cc'.
params.rho_unit: Density unit switch: 'kg/m3' (default, SI) or 'g/cc' for g/cm3, the CBL articles' convention.
params.v: Acoustic (compressional) velocity of the medium in m/s.

acoustic_impedance.returns: Acoustic impedance Z = rho*v expressed in MRayl, as a numpy float64 array broadcast from the inputs.

params.a0: Initial (source-side) amplitude A0 in the exponential decay A = A0*exp(-alpha*x), any amplitude units.
params.ax: Amplitude measured after propagating the distance x_m, in the same units as a0.
params.x_m: Propagation distance in metres over which the amplitude decayed from a0 to ax.

attenuation_coefficient.returns: Exponential attenuation coefficient alpha = ln(A0/Ax)/x in 1/m, as a numpy float64 array broadcast from the inputs.

params.a_far: Signal amplitude at the far receiver, in the same units as a_near.
params.a_near: Signal amplitude at the near receiver, in any consistent amplitude units.
params.spacing_m: Optional receiver spacing in metres; when given, the decibel value is divided by it to yield dB/m.

attenuation_db.returns: Amplitude attenuation 20*log10(A_near/A_far) as a numpy float64 array, in dB, or in dB/m when spacing_m is provided.

params.clip: Two-element (low, high) bounds to which the computed bond index is clipped, default (0.0, 1.0).
params.free_pipe: Anchor value of the measurement in fully unbonded (free) pipe, corresponding to bond index 0.
params.input_kind: Measurement type: 'amplitude' (falls as bond improves) or 'attenuation' (rises as bond improves; linear method only).
params.measured: The CBL measurement to convert, either an amplitude or an attenuation value depending on input_kind.
params.method: Conversion convention: 'linear' interpolates between the two anchors; 'log' uses the logarithmic CBL formula log(free_pipe/A)/log(free_pipe/well_bonded) with 0.01 amplitude floors, amplitude input only.
params.well_bonded: Anchor value of the measurement in fully bonded pipe, corresponding to bond index 1.

bond_index.returns: Cement bond index as a numpy float64 array clipped to the clip bounds, equal to 0 at the free-pipe anchor and 1 at the well-bonded anchor.

params.bi_a: First bond indicator (e.g. a bond index from one measurement), scaled by weights[0].
params.bi_b: Second bond indicator, scaled by weights[1].
params.corrector: Optional callable applied to the raw weighted combination before clipping, e.g. an eccentricity-correction model.
params.weights: Two-element weights applied to bi_a and bi_b in the linear combination, default (0.6, 0.4).

bond_index_combined.returns: Combined bond index as a numpy float64 array, clipped to [0, 1].

params.A: Bowers loading-curve amplitude coefficient in V = V0 + A*sigma^B (default 10.0).
params.U: Bowers unloading exponent (default 3.0), used only when unloading=True.
params.sigma_max: Maximum past effective stress in psi anchoring the unloading branch (default 5000.0).
params.unloading: If True, uses the flatter unloading branch anchored at sigma_max with exponent U; default False uses the virgin loading curve.
params.v0: Mudline (zero effective stress) velocity V0 in ft/s (default 5000.0).

bowers_pore_pressure.returns: Bowers pore pressure in psi as a numpy float64 array: the overburden minus the effective stress inverted from the velocity; on the loading branch the term (V - V0)/A is floored at 0.01 before raising to 1/B.

params.bands: Three ascending band edges in percent (default (10.0, 25.0, 42.5)) separating good/fair, fair/poor, and poor/critical; comparison is strict less-than at each edge.
params.loss_pct: Casing metal loss in percent of nominal wall thickness.

casing_condition.returns: Numpy array of string condition labels 'good', 'fair', 'poor', or 'critical' matching the shape of loss_pct.

params.correction: Multiplicative mode-correction factor (default 1.0; e.g. 0.95 for the S1 negative-group-velocity minimum).
params.n: Harmonic number of the thickness resonance (default 1, the fundamental).
params.v: Compressional velocity of the casing material in m/s (default 5900.0, casing steel).

casing_resonance_frequency.returns: Casing plate thickness-resonance frequency f = correction*n*v/(2*d) in Hz, as a numpy float64 array.

params.correction: Multiplicative mode-correction factor (default 1.0), the same factor used in the forward resonance formula.
params.n: Harmonic number of the observed resonance (default 1, the fundamental).
params.v: Compressional velocity of the casing material in m/s (default 5900.0, casing steel).

casing_thickness_from_resonance.returns: Casing wall thickness correction*n*v/(2*f) in metres, as a numpy float64 array.

params.cement_type: Cement type key selecting the default (good, fair) thresholds: 'Portland' (4.0, 2.5), 'CO2_resistant' (3.5, 2.0), or 'epoxy_resin' (2.5, 1.5) MRayl.
params.thresholds: Optional explicit (good, fair) impedance thresholds in MRayl overriding the cement_type defaults.

cement_quality_score.returns: Continuous cement-quality score as a numpy float64 array clipped to [0, 1]: 1 at or above the good threshold, ramping 0.5 to 1 between fair and good, and 0.25*Z/fair below fair.

classify_annulus.returns: Class label string: 'gas', 'liquid', 'cement', or 'transition' for impedances between liquid_max and cement_min.

params.medium_max: Relative amplitude at or below which (above good_max) the cement is classed 'Medium' (default 0.30).
params.relative_amp: CBL amplitude relative to the free-pipe amplitude (dimensionless fraction); lower values indicate better cement bond.

classify_cement_from_cbl.returns: Numpy array of string class labels 'Good', 'Medium', or 'Poor' matching the shape of relative_amp.

classify_fluid_haworth.returns: Fluid class label string. With n_classes=4 one of 'dry gas', 'gas', 'gas-condensate', 'oil', 'residual oil'; with n_classes=8 one of 'no gas', 'very dry gas', 'dry gas', 'wet gas', 'gas condensate', 'volatile oil', 'light oil', 'medium/black oil', 'heavy/residual oil'.

drilling_window_margin.returns: Drilling-window width frac - pore as a numpy float64 array, in the same units as the inputs; positive where a window exists.

params.tvd_m: True vertical depth of the fluid column in metres.

params.tvd_m: True vertical depth of the fluid column in metres.

params.t_s: Elapsed filtration time(s) in seconds; for model='chin_ode' this must be an increasing time array over which the ODE is stepped.
params.v: Decay exponent of the cake permeability K(t) = k_mc*((t + 1e-3)/1e-3)^-v in the chin_ode model (default 0.5).

mudcake_thickness.returns: Mudcake thickness in metres as a numpy float64 array matching t_s; the chin_ode model starts from a 1e-5 m (10-micron) seed cake and integrates forward in time.

params.bit_diameter: Bit diameter in inches, used for the borehole cross-sectional area in the metric formula; unused when units='field'.
params.flow: Mud flow rate: L/min for units='metric', gpm for units='field'.
params.mud_weight: Mud weight in ppg, required for units='field' (enters the correction as the squared ratio to the reference); ignored for 'metric'.
params.reference: Reference (ROP0, Q0, MW0) drilling conditions for the field normalization, default (30.0 ft/hr, 500.0 gpm, 10.0 ppg).
params.rop: Rate of penetration: m/hr for units='metric' (floored at 1e-6), ft/hr for units='field'.
params.total_gas: Total mud-gas reading to normalize, in any consistent gas units.
params.units: Normalization convention: 'metric' returns the drilled-rock gas index G*Q/(ROP*A_bit); 'field' divides the reading by the factors (ROP/ROP0)*(Q/Q0)*(MW/MW0)^2 to bring it to reference conditions.

normalize_gas.returns: Normalized gas as a numpy float64 array: the drilled-rock gas index for units='metric', or the reading corrected to the reference drilling conditions (same units as total_gas) for units='field'.

params.rho_bulk: Constant bulk density of the sediment column in kg/m3.
params.tvd_m: True vertical depth below the mudline (sediment column thickness) in metres.
params.water_depth_m: Seawater column thickness in metres (default 0.0, no water column).

overburden_pressure.returns: Overburden pressure g*(rho_sw*water_depth + rho_bulk*tvd) in Pa, with g = 9.80665 m/s^2, as a numpy float64 array.

params.sediment_depth_ft: Sediment column thickness (depth below mudline) in feet.

overburden_pressure_psi.returns: Deepwater overburden pressure 0.433*(SG_sw*water_depth + SG_sed*sediment_depth) in psi, as a numpy float64 array.

params.c1: Methane (C1) mud-gas reading, the numerator of every Pixler ratio; any consistent gas units.
params.c2: Ethane (C2) mud-gas reading, same units as the other components.
params.c3: Propane (C3) mud-gas reading, same units as the other components.
params.c4: Butane (C4) mud-gas reading, same units as the other components.
params.c5: Pentane (C5) mud-gas reading, same units as the other components.

pixler_ratios.returns: Dict of scalar floats with keys 'C1/C2', 'C1/C3', 'C1/C4', 'C1/C5' (Pixler component ratios) and 'bernard' (the Bernard ratio C1/(C2+C3)); zero denominators give +inf.

params.z1: Acoustic impedance of the incident-side medium, in the same units as z2 (e.g. MRayl).
params.z2: Acoustic impedance of the transmitted-side medium, in the same units as z1.

reflection_coefficient.returns: Dimensionless normal-incidence reflection coefficient R = (Z2 - Z1)/(Z2 + Z1), a numpy float64 array broadcast from the inputs, ranging between -1 and 1.

params.min_acceptable_mm: Minimum acceptable wall thickness in millimetres, below which the casing is no longer serviceable.
params.rate_mm_per_yr: Corrosion (wall-loss) rate in millimetres per year; a non-positive value is treated as no wear.
params.thickness_mm: Current casing wall thickness in millimetres.

remaining_life_years.returns: Remaining casing life (thickness - minimum)/rate in years as a scalar float, floored at 0; a non-positive corrosion rate returns the 999.0 sentinel.

params.z1: Acoustic impedance of the incident-side medium, in the same units as z2 (e.g. MRayl).
params.z2: Acoustic impedance of the transmitted-side medium, in the same units as z1.

transmission_energy.returns: Fraction of incident acoustic energy transmitted across the interface, 1 - R^2, as a dimensionless numpy float64 array in [0, 1].

params.ecd: Equivalent circulating density or downhole pressure, in the same units as pore and frac.
params.frac: Fracture pressure, the upper window bound, same units as ecd.
params.pore: Pore pressure, the lower window bound, same units as ecd.

within_drilling_window.returns: Boolean scalar: True when pore < ecd < frac, i.e. the ECD sits strictly inside the drilling window, otherwise False.

params.kind: Misfit measure: l2 weighted sum of squares, rms root-mean-square, rel_data per-datum relative squared misfit, rel_norm relative 2-norm, chi2 weighted sum of squares with weights 1/sigma^2.
params.log_space: If True, compares log10 of the simulated and observed values instead of the raw values.
params.obs: Observed (measured) data values the simulation is compared against.
params.sim: Simulated (forward-modelled) data values, as an array matching the observations.
params.weights: Optional per-datum weights w applied to the squared residuals; defaults to ones, and for kind chi2 should be 1/sigma^2.

misfit.returns: Scalar float data misfit between simulation and observations, computed by the selected kind; rel_data and rel_norm are dimensionless, the others carry the (squared) units of the data.

params.a: Forward or design matrix A of the linear system A x = b being regularized.
params.b: Observed data vector b of the linear system.
params.bracket: Tuple (low, high) bounding the lam search; bisection proceeds geometrically on log(lam) inside it (default (1e-6, 1e3)).
params.chi2_target: Target data misfit ||A x_lam - b||^2 that the discrepancy principle should reach, e.g. n*sigma^2.
params.max_iter: Number of geometric bisection iterations performed (default 50).

reg_lambda_brd.returns: Scalar float Tikhonov regularization weight lam whose data misfit matches chi2_target, found by geometric bisection within the bracket.

params.alpha: Multiplicative coefficient of the schedule lam = alpha * misfit^beta.
params.beta: Exponent applied to the misfit value in the cooling schedule.
params.lam_max: Upper cap on the returned regularization weight; defaults to infinity (no cap).
params.misfit_value: Current data-misfit value driving the cooling schedule.

reg_lambda_multiplicative.returns: Scalar float regularization weight lam = min(alpha * misfit_value**beta, lam_max), the Habashy-Abubakar multiplicative cooling value.

params.az: Azimuth angles of the samples, in degrees.
params.y: Measured values at each azimuth to be fit by the cosine harmonic.

fit_cosine.returns: Tuple (mean, amplitude, phase_deg) of floats for the fit y = mean + amplitude*cos(az - phase): mean level and amplitude in the units of y, phase in degrees.

params.t: Time (independent-variable) samples of the relaxation record.
params.three_point: If True, uses the closed-form estimate from the first, middle, and last samples (assumes equal spacing); if False (default), fits with scipy curve_fit.
params.y: Observed values approaching an asymptote as y = asymptote + (y0 - asymptote)*exp(-t/tau).

fit_exponential_approach.returns: Tuple (asymptote, tau) of floats: the long-time asymptote in the units of y and the relaxation time constant tau in the units of t.

params.x: Independent-variable samples of the regression.
params.xform: Optional transform applied to x before fitting: None, log10, log, inv, sqrt, or square.
params.y: Dependent-variable samples, same length as x.
params.yform: Optional transform applied to y before fitting; same accepted names as xform.

fit_line.returns: LineFit named tuple (slope, intercept, r2) of floats from the degree-1 least-squares fit, all reported in the transformed space; r2 is the coefficient of determination (0 when y is constant).

params.exponent: Optional known decay exponent b; when given only the amplitude a is recovered by linear projection, otherwise both a and b are fit with scipy curve_fit.
params.x: Independent-variable samples of the decay record.
params.y: Observed decaying values to be fit by y = a * x**(-b).

fit_powerlaw_decay.returns: Tuple (a, b) of floats for the model y = a * x**(-b): the amplitude a in the units of y and the decay exponent b (dimensionless).

params.a: Matrix whose numerical conditioning is assessed.

condition_number.returns: Scalar float 2-norm condition number of the matrix, the ratio of its largest to smallest singular value.

params.kernel: 1-D convolution (blurring) kernel, treated as centered on each output sample.
params.n: Signal length, i.e. the side of the square output matrix.

convolution_matrix.returns: Numpy float array of shape (n, n): the convolution matrix G whose rows apply the centered kernel, each row renormalized to sum to one so edge truncation is compensated.

params.d: Observed (blurred) data vector, modelled as d = G x.
params.g: Convolution/blurring matrix G, e.g. built with convolution_matrix.
params.rank: Optional SVD truncation rank for the solve; None (default) uses the full Moore-Penrose pseudo-inverse.

deconvolve.returns: Numpy float array x, the deconvolved signal estimate from the (rank-limited) pseudo-inverse of G applied to d.

params.n: Number of model parameters, i.e. the number of columns of the operator.
params.order: Difference order: 0 returns the (n, n) identity, 1 (default) the first-difference operator, 2 the second difference, and so on.

difference_operator.returns: Numpy float array L of shape (n - order, n) (or the (n, n) identity for order 0) that applies the finite-difference roughness operator to a length-n model vector.

params.a: Coefficient matrix A of the sparse inversion.
params.b: Data vector b to be fit.
params.eta: L1 sparsity weight in the objective 0.5*||Ax - b||^2 + eta*||x||_1 (default 0.1).
params.max_iter: Number of ISTA soft-thresholding iterations performed (default 500).
params.nonneg: If True (default), each iterate is additionally projected onto x >= 0.

ista_l1.returns: Numpy float array x, the sparse solution after max_iter iterative soft-thresholding steps, with length equal to the number of columns of A.

params.ell: Optional prior regularization operator L; the identity when omitted.
params.g: Linear forward operator (sensitivity matrix) G mapping the model vector to data.
params.m_prior: Optional prior mean model the estimate is regularized toward; zero when omitted.
params.noise_var: Scalar data-noise variance dividing the G^T G and G^T d terms of the posterior precision.
params.prior_strength: Scalar weight of the prior precision term prior_strength * L^T L.

map_estimate.returns: Tuple (x, posterior_cov): the Bayesian MAP model estimate as a numpy float array of length n_param, and the (n_param, n_param) posterior covariance matrix A^{-1} where A = G^T G/noise_var + prior_strength*L^T L.

params.a: Coefficient matrix of the least-squares system.
params.b: Right-hand-side data vector.
params.reg: Ridge weight; when greater than 0 the system is augmented with sqrt(reg)*I rows pulling the solution toward zero (default 0.0).

nnls_solve.returns: Numpy float array x with all entries >= 0, the non-negative least-squares solution (scipy.optimize.nnls) of the optionally ridge-augmented system.

params.total: Required sum of the projected vector (default 1.0).
params.v: Input vector to be projected onto the simplex.

project_simplex.returns: Numpy float array, the Euclidean projection of v onto the simplex {x >= 0, sum(x) = total}, same length as v.

params.a: Coefficient matrix A of the linear system A x = b.
params.b: Right-hand-side data vector b.
params.rank: Optional number of singular values kept in the pseudo-inverse; None (default) keeps them all (plain least-squares solve).

svd_solve.returns: Numpy float array x, the truncated-SVD (rank-limited pseudo-inverse) least-squares solution of A x = b, with length equal to the number of columns of A.

params.a: Forward or design matrix A of the regularized least-squares problem.
params.b: Data vector b to be fit.
params.lam: Regularization weight multiplying the penalty ||L(x - x_ref)||^2, applied once and never squared (default 1.0).
params.nonneg: If True, solves the lam-augmented stacked system with non-negative least squares (nnls_solve) instead of the normal equations.
params.reg_op: Optional regularization operator L (e.g. from difference_operator); the identity when omitted.
params.sigma: Optional per-datum standard deviations; rows of A and b are weighted by 1/sigma.
params.x_ref: Optional reference model the solution is pulled toward by the penalty; zero when omitted.

tikhonov_solve.returns: Numpy float array x minimizing ||W(Ax - b)||^2 + lam*||L(x - x_ref)||^2, with length equal to the number of columns of A; non-negative when nonneg=True.

params.closure: Sum-to-one handling: None (no closure), 'row' appends a heavily weighted unit-sum equation to the system, 'simplex' projects the final solution onto the unit simplex.
params.measured: Measured log or spectral values to be unmixed (the data vector).
params.nonneg: Non-negativity handling: None plain least squares, 'clip' clips negatives to zero, 'nnls' (default) solves with non-negative least squares, 'simplex' projects the least-squares solution onto the unit simplex.
params.normalize: If True, rescales the final solution to sum to one (skipped when the sum is zero).
params.response_matrix: End-member response matrix; each column is one component's response, so measured is modelled as response_matrix @ x.
params.sigma: Optional per-measurement standard deviations applying 1/sigma weights to the rows of the system.

unmix.returns: Numpy float array x of component fractions or abundances, one entry per response-matrix column, satisfying the requested non-negativity and closure options.

params.f: Scalar objective callable evaluated at a parameter vector.
params.x: Parameter vector at which the gradient is taken.

fd_gradient.returns: Numpy float array of length len(x): the finite-difference gradient of the scalar objective, computed via fd_jacobian (extra keyword arguments such as eps or scheme are passed through).

params.eps: Finite-difference step size (default 1e-6); scaled per parameter by max(|m_i|, 1) when relative is True.
params.forward: Forward-model callable mapping a model vector to a data vector.
params.m: Model vector at which the Jacobian is evaluated.
params.relative: If True (default), the perturbation per parameter is eps*max(|m_i|, 1); if False, the absolute step eps is used.
params.scheme: 'central' (default) uses central differences; 'forward' uses one-sided forward differences.

fd_jacobian.returns: Numpy float array of shape (n_data, n_param) holding the finite-difference Jacobian d forward / d m.

params.bounds: List of (low, high) tuples per parameter; candidates are clipped into this box.
params.forward: Forward-model callable mapping candidate models to predicted data.
params.m_center: Central solution around which random candidate models are perturbed.
params.n_samples: Number of random candidate models drawn (default 2000).
params.noise_level: Data noise level; a candidate is accepted when its RMS data residual is within 2*noise_level.
params.step_frac: Standard deviation of the perturbation as a fraction of each parameter's bound range (default 0.15).

feasible_set_sampling.returns: Dict with keys 'P5', 'P50', 'P95' holding per-parameter percentile arrays of the accepted models, and 'models' holding the (n_accepted, n_param) array of accepted models (the center alone if none were accepted).

params.data: Observed data vector the predictions are compared to.
params.forward: Forward-model callable evaluated at every grid combination.
params.grids: List of 1-D candidate-value arrays, one per parameter; their Cartesian product is searched exhaustively.
params.misfit: 'log_l2' (default) sums squared differences of natural logs of prediction and data; any other value uses the plain L2 sum of squares.

grid_search.returns: Tuple (best, best_err): the minimizing parameter combination as a numpy float array and its scalar float misfit value.

params.bounds: Optional (lo, hi) pair of arrays box-constraining the physical model; iterates are clipped into the box.
params.damping: 'diag' (default) damps with the diagonal of J^T J (Marquardt scaling); 'identity' damps with the identity matrix (Levenberg).
params.data: Observed data vector the model is fit to.
params.forward: Forward-model callable mapping a physical model vector to predicted data.
params.lam_dn: Factor multiplying the damping after an accepted step (default 0.5).
params.lam_up: Factor multiplying the damping after a rejected (cost-increasing) step (default 5.0).
params.log_params: If True, the inversion works on log(m), keeping parameters positive and making steps multiplicative.
params.tol: Convergence tolerance on the decrease of the squared-residual cost between accepted steps (default 1e-8).

levenberg_marquardt.returns: InvResult named tuple: model (final physical model, numpy float array), misfit (final sum of squared residuals, float), n_iter (iterations used), converged (bool), and lam (final damping value).

params.aggregate: 'best' returns the lowest-misfit model; 'misfit_weighted' (default) returns the softmax(-misfit) weighted average of all recovered models.
params.bounds: List of (low, high) tuples per parameter defining the box from which uniform random starts are drawn.
params.seed: Seed of the random-number generator drawing the start points (default 0).
params.solver: Callable run from each random start; must return an InvResult or a (model, misfit) tuple.

multistart.returns: Numpy float array, the aggregated model over all starts: either the single best model or the misfit-weighted average, length n_param.

params.forward: Forward-model callable mapping the model vector to predicted data.
params.lam0: Initial roughness weight lam (default 100.0), multiplied by 0.8 each iteration until the chi-square target is reached.
params.noise_level: Data noise standard deviation; residuals are scaled by 1/noise_level and the chi-square target equals the number of data.
params.reg_order: Order of the finite-difference roughness operator L penalizing ||L m||^2 (default 2, second difference).

occam.returns: Numpy float array, the smoothest model found that fits the data to within the noise level (chi-square <= number of data), or the model after max_iter iterations.

params.backtracking: If True, the step is halved each iteration until an Armijo sufficient-decrease condition holds; if False (default), the fixed lr is used.
params.lr: Learning rate (step size); None uses the default 1e-2, which is also the starting step for backtracking.
params.tol: Stopping tolerance on the absolute change of the objective between iterations (default 1e-10).

gradient_descent.returns: Numpy float array, the parameter vector at termination (after convergence to tol or max_iter finite-difference gradient steps).

params.bounds: List of (low, high) tuples per dimension defining the search box; particle positions are clipped into it.
params.c1: Cognitive acceleration coefficient pulling each particle toward its personal best (default 1.5).
params.c2: Social acceleration coefficient pulling each particle toward the global best (default 1.5).
params.inertia_decay: Optional factor multiplying the inertia weight after every iteration; None (default) keeps it constant.
params.n_particles: Number of particles in the swarm (default 30).
params.objective: Scalar objective callable to minimize, evaluated at a parameter vector.
params.omega: Inertia weight multiplying each particle's previous velocity (default 0.7).
params.seed: Seed of the random-number generator initializing positions and random factors (default 0).

pso.returns: Tuple (x_best, f_best, history): the best parameter vector found (numpy float array), its scalar float objective value, and a numpy array of the best objective per iteration (length n_iter + 1).

params.alpha: Diffusivity of the explicit scheme, in consistent length-squared-per-time units.
params.dt: Time-step size, in the time unit consistent with alpha.
params.dx: Grid spacing, in the length unit consistent with alpha.

cfl_number.returns: Scalar float diffusion CFL number alpha*dt/dx^2 (dimensionless); explicit stability requires a value <= 0.5.

params.bc: Boundary condition: 'neumann' (default) applies no-flux ends, 'dirichlet' holds the two endpoint values fixed.
params.dt: Time-step size of the explicit update.
params.source: Optional per-node source term added to the field as dt*source; None (default) means no source.
params.u: Current 1-D field values on the grid.

diffusion_step_1d.returns: Numpy float array of the field after one explicit finite-difference diffusion step u + alpha*dt/dx^2 * laplacian(u) + dt*source, same length as u.

params.n_iter: Maximum number of Jacobi relaxation sweeps (default 5000).
params.sigma_map: 2-D array (ny, nx) of cell conductivities; current is driven vertically from the top row (V=1) to the bottom row (V=0) with no-flux sides.
params.tol: Convergence tolerance on the maximum potential change between sweeps (default 1e-7).

effective_conductivity_2d.returns: Scalar float effective vertical conductivity of the map, in the same units as sigma_map, computed from the top-layer current after solving the steady Laplace problem div(sigma grad V) = 0 with harmonic-mean face conductivities.

params.ens: Prior ensemble of state/model vectors, shape (n_ens, n_state).
params.obs_cov: Observation-error covariance matrix R, used both in the Kalman gain and to perturb the observations per member.
params.obs_op: Observation operator mapping state to observation space: either a callable state -> obs, or a matrix H applied as x @ H.T.
params.seed: Seed of the random-number generator drawing the observation perturbations (default 0).

enkf_update.returns: Numpy float array of shape (n_ens, n_state): the analysis (updated) ensemble after the stochastic perturbed-observation ensemble-Kalman update.

params.log_space: If True, natural logs of obs and pred are taken before differencing.
params.pred: Predicted (modelled) data values compared to the observations.
params.sigma: Data standard deviation(s) scaling the residuals; scalar or per-datum array.
params.weights: Optional per-datum weights on the squared standardized residuals; defaults to ones.

gaussian_loglik.returns: Scalar float Gaussian log-likelihood -0.5*sum(w*((obs - pred)/sigma)^2); the normalization constant is omitted.

params.forward: Forward-model callable mapping a model vector to predicted data.
params.lam_dn: Factor multiplying lam after an accepted update (default 0.4).
params.lam_up: Factor multiplying lam after a rejected (misfit-increasing) update (default 4.0).
params.obs_cov: Observation-error covariance matrix R, inflated by (1 + lam) in the update and used for per-member observation perturbations.
params.prior_cov: Prior covariance matrix, used both to sample the initial ensemble and inside the update gain.
params.prior_mean: Mean of the Gaussian prior over the model parameters, used to sample the initial ensemble.

lm_enrml.returns: Numpy float array of shape (n_ens, n_param): the final ensemble of model vectors after the damped Kalman-type updates with SVD-based empirical sensitivity.

params.log_post: Callable returning the log-posterior density of a parameter vector; its finite-difference gradient supplies the Langevin drift.
params.n_samples: Number of MALA iterations; every state is kept (no burn-in).
params.step: Langevin step size; proposals are x + 0.5*step^2*grad(log_post) + step*N(0,1).

mala.returns: Chain named tuple: samples is the (n_samples, n_param) numpy array of chain states and acceptance the accepted-move fraction (float).

params.burn_in: Number of initial iterations excluded from the returned samples (default 0).
params.log_post: Callable returning the log-posterior density of a parameter vector.
params.log_space: If True, proposals are multiplicative x*exp(step*N(0,1)), keeping parameters positive; if False (default), additive x + step*N(0,1).
params.n_samples: Total number of MCMC iterations run (default 4000).
params.step: Proposal scale per parameter (scalar or array): the additive standard deviation, or the multiplicative log-scale when log_space is True.
params.x0: Starting point of the Markov chain.

metropolis.returns: Chain named tuple: samples is the (n_samples - burn_in, n_param) numpy array of retained chain states, acceptance is the fraction of accepted moves over all iterations (float).

params.x: Parameter vector to evaluate.

soft_envelope_logprior.returns: Scalar float log-prior: 0 for x inside [lo, hi] and the quadratic penalty -0.5*sum(excess^2) growing with the distance outside the envelope.

params.bounds: List of (low, high) tuples, one per parameter, defining the uniform box support.
params.x: Parameter vector to evaluate against the box.

uniform_logprior.returns: Scalar float log-prior: 0.0 when every component of x lies inside its bounds, -inf otherwise.

params.y_pred: Predicted class labels, same shape as y_true.

accuracy.returns: Scalar float fraction of samples whose predicted label matches the true label, in [0, 1].

params.src_hi: Source-range upper endpoint that maps to dst_hi.
params.src_lo: Source-range lower endpoint (e.g. a reference percentile) that maps to dst_lo.
params.x: Input values to map.

affine_rescale.returns: Numpy float64 array with the linear map dst_lo + (x - src_lo)*(dst_hi - dst_lo)/(src_hi - src_lo) applied; values outside the source range extrapolate linearly (no clipping).

params.labels: Optional explicit class ordering for the matrix rows and columns; default None uses the sorted union of labels found in y_true and y_pred.
params.y_pred: Predicted class labels, same length as y_true.

confusion_matrix.returns: Square numpy int64 matrix where C[i, j] counts samples of true class i predicted as class j; for binary 0/1 labels this is the [[TN, FP], [FN, TP]] layout.

params.x: Independent-variable sample values.
params.y: Dependent-variable sample values, same length as x.

fit_line.returns: Tuple of two floats (slope, intercept) of the degree-1 least-squares fit y = slope*x + intercept, always in that order.

params.x: Independent-variable values; must be positive, since zeros or negatives propagate as NaN through the log10 transform.
params.y: Dependent-variable values; must be positive for the same reason.

fit_powerlaw.returns: Tuple of two floats (coefficient, exponent) such that y is approximately coefficient * x**exponent, obtained from a log10-log10 line fit.

params.max_iter: Maximum number of Lloyd iterations (default 100); iteration stops early when the labels no longer change.
params.weights: Optional per-sample weights used in the weighted-mean centroid update; default None gives equal weights.
params.x: Data matrix of shape (n_samples, n_features) to cluster.

kmeans.returns: Tuple (labels, centroids): a numpy int64 array of per-sample cluster indices and a (k, n_features) numpy float64 array of cluster centers. Deterministic by construction (farthest-point initialization, no RNG); cluster numbering is an artifact of the initialization, not a physical ordering.

params.y_pred: Model-predicted values, same shape as y_true.

mae.returns: Scalar float mean absolute error mean(|y_pred - y_true|), in the units of the target.

params.as_percent: If True (default) the mean absolute relative error is multiplied by 100 and returned as a percentage; False returns the raw fraction.
params.y_pred: Model-predicted values, same shape as y_true.
params.y_true: Observed (true) target values; division by them is deliberately unguarded, so a zero observation yields inf/NaN.

mape.returns: Scalar float mean absolute percentage error (the articles' AAPE), as a percentage by default or a fraction with as_percent=False; inf/NaN when any observed value is zero.

params.axis: None (default) uses the global min and max; an integer axis rescales independently along that axis.
params.eps: Additive guard added to the (max - min) range denominator; the default 0.0 yields NaN for a constant input.
params.hi: Upper endpoint of the output range (default 1.0).
params.lo: Lower endpoint of the output range (default 0.0).
params.x: Input array to rescale.

minmax.returns: Numpy float64 array of the same shape, linearly rescaled so the data minimum maps to lo and the maximum to hi.

params.X: Design matrix of shape (n_samples, n_features); a 1-D vector is treated as a single feature column.
params.intercept: If True (default) an intercept term is fitted via an appended ones column; if False the model is forced through the origin and 0.0 is returned as the intercept.

ols.returns: Tuple (coef, intercept): a numpy float64 array of one coefficient per feature, and a scalar float intercept returned separately (0.0 when intercept=False), avoiding the beta[0]-vs-coef[-1] position trap.

params.n_components: Number of principal components to retain; default None keeps all components.
params.x: Data matrix of shape (n_samples, n_features).

pca.returns: Tuple of four numpy float64 arrays (scores, components, mean, explained): the projected data of shape (n_samples, n_kept), the principal axes as rows of shape (n_kept, n_features), the per-feature mean used for centering, and the fraction of total variance explained by each retained component.

params.eps: Additive guard added to the product-of-standard-deviations denominator; the default 0.0 gives NaN when either input is constant.
params.x: First variable's sample values.
params.y: Second variable's sample values, same length as x.

pearson_r.returns: Scalar float Pearson correlation coefficient R between x and y, in [-1, 1] (not R squared); NaN for a constant input with eps=0.

params.positive: Label value treated as the positive class (default 1).
params.y_pred: Predicted class labels, same length as y_true.
params.zero_division: Value returned for any of the three metrics whose denominator is zero (default 0.0, the repository convention).

precision_recall_f1.returns: Tuple of three floats (precision, recall, f1) for the positive class, each replaced by zero_division when its denominator is empty.

pnc_decay.returns: Pulsed-neutron capture count rate N(t) = N0*exp(-Sigma*v*t) + background at each time, using thermal-neutron velocity v = 2.2e5 cm/s, in the same count units as n0; numpy float64 array.

params.half_life: Half-life T_half of the species, in the same time units as t.
params.n0: Initial quantity N0 (e.g. number of atoms or count rate) at time zero.
params.t: Elapsed time, in the same time units as half_life.

radioactive_decay.returns: Remaining quantity N = N0*exp(-ln(2)*t/T_half), in the same units as n0; numpy float64 array.

rhob_from_rhoe.returns: Apparent bulk density rho_b = 1.0704*rho_e - 0.1883 in g/cc, the standard gamma-gamma tool transform; numpy float64 array.

rhoe_from_rhob.returns: Electron density rho_e = (rho_b + 0.1883)/1.0704 in g/cc-equivalent units, the inverse of rhob_from_rhoe; numpy float64 array.

params.phi: Porosity as a volume fraction of the bulk rock (0-1).
params.sigma_hc: Capture cross-section of the hydrocarbon phase in c.u., default 21.0.
params.sigma_ma: Capture cross-section of the clean matrix in capture units (c.u.), default 10.0.
params.sigma_sh: Capture cross-section of the shale fraction in c.u., default 27.0.
params.sigma_w: Capture cross-section of the formation water in c.u., default 55.0.
params.sw: Water saturation as a fraction of the pore volume (0-1).
params.vsh: Shale volume fraction of the bulk rock (0-1); default 0.0 gives the clean two-phase form.

sigma_forward.returns: Volumetric bulk capture cross-section Sigma of the rock in capture units (c.u., 1 c.u. = 1e-3 per cm), as a numpy float64 array broadcast over the inputs.

params.phi: Porosity as a volume fraction of the bulk rock (0-1).
params.sg: Gas saturation as a fraction of the pore volume (0-1).
params.sigma_gas: Capture cross-section of the gas phase in c.u., default 8.0.
params.sigma_ma: Capture cross-section of the matrix in c.u., default 10.0.
params.sigma_oil: Capture cross-section of the oil phase in c.u., default 22.0.
params.sigma_w: Capture cross-section of the formation water in c.u., default 80.0.
params.so: Oil saturation as a fraction of the pore volume (0-1).
params.sw: Water saturation as a fraction of the pore volume (0-1).

sigma_forward_3phase.returns: Three-phase (oil, gas, water) volumetric bulk capture cross-section Sigma in c.u., as a numpy float64 array broadcast over the inputs.

params.counts: Measured counts at each sample time; must be positive because the fit is on ln(counts).
params.fit_window: Optional (start, stop) integer index pair selecting the sample slice used for the fit; None fits all samples.
params.t_us: Sample times of the measured decay curve in microseconds.

sigma_from_decay_fit.returns: Capture cross-section Sigma in c.u., obtained from the slope of a log-linear fit of ln(counts) versus time via Sigma = -slope/v * 1e3 with v the thermal-neutron velocity; a single numpy float64 value.

params.tau_us: Thermal-neutron decay time constant tau in microseconds.

sigma_from_tau.returns: Capture cross-section Sigma = 4550/tau in c.u.; numpy float64 array.

params.phi: Porosity as a volume fraction of the bulk rock (0-1).
params.sigma_hc: Capture cross-section of the hydrocarbon phase in c.u.
params.sigma_w: Capture cross-section of the formation water in c.u.

sigma_sensitivity.returns: Saturation sensitivity dSigma/dSw = phi*(Sigma_w - Sigma_hc) in c.u. per unit change in water saturation; numpy float64 array.

params.model: Correlation selector: 'fitz2023' uses (22 + 750*s)*(1 - 8e-4*(T-75)) with s = ppm/1e6; 'linear450k' interpolates linearly from 22 to 220 c.u. at 450,000 ppm; other values raise ValueError.
params.ppm_nacl: NaCl salinity of the brine in parts per million.
params.temperature_c: Brine temperature in degrees Celsius used by the fitz2023 model, default 75.0; ignored by the linear450k model.

sigma_w_from_salinity.returns: Brine capture cross-section Sigma_w in c.u. for the chosen correlation; numpy float64 array.

params.e_ref: Reference neutron energy in MeV, default 4.5.
params.ls0: Reference slowing-down length in cm at zero porosity and the reference energy, default 20.0.
params.phi: Porosity as a volume fraction of the bulk rock (0-1).

slowing_down_length_empirical.returns: Empirical neutron slowing-down length Ls = Ls0*(1 - 0.6*phi)*sqrt(E/E_ref) in cm; numpy float64 array.

params.clip: Tuple (low, high) of bounds applied to the interpolated saturation with numpy clip, default (0.0, 1.0); pass None to return the unclipped value.
params.cor_oil: C/O ratio endpoint corresponding to 100 percent oil saturation.
params.cor_water: C/O ratio endpoint corresponding to 100 percent water saturation.

so_from_co.returns: Oil saturation So = (COR - COR_w)/(COR_o - COR_w) as a fraction of the pore volume by linear endpoint interpolation, clipped to (0, 1) by default; numpy float64 array.

params.rho_ls: Long-spacing detector apparent density in g/cc.
params.rho_ss: Short-spacing detector apparent density in g/cc.
params.rib_coeffs: Tuple (c0, c1, c2) of rib-polynomial coefficients giving drho = c0 + c1*d + c2*d**2 with d = rho_ls - rho_ss; default (0.0, 1.0, 0.0).

spine_ribs.returns: Tuple of two numpy float64 arrays (rho_b, drho): the compensated bulk density rho_b = rho_ls + drho in g/cc, and the density correction drho in g/cc from the rib polynomial.

params.clip: Tuple (low, high) of bounds applied to the computed saturation with numpy clip, default (0.0, 1.0); pass None to skip clipping.
params.phi: Porosity as a volume fraction of the bulk rock (0-1).
params.sigma_hc: Capture cross-section of the hydrocarbon phase in c.u. (required keyword).
params.sigma_ma: Capture cross-section of the clean matrix in c.u. (required keyword).
params.sigma_sh: Capture cross-section of the shale fraction in c.u., default 27.0.
params.sigma_t: Measured total formation capture cross-section Sigma in c.u.
params.sigma_w: Capture cross-section of the formation water in c.u. (required keyword).
params.vsh: Shale volume fraction of the bulk rock (0-1); default 0.0 for the clean form.

sw_from_sigma.returns: Water saturation Sw as a fraction of the pore volume, obtained by inverting the volumetric Sigma balance and clipped to the clip bounds by default; numpy float64 array.

tau_from_sigma.returns: Thermal-neutron decay time tau = 4550/Sigma in microseconds; numpy float64 array.

params.calib: Calibration multiplier converting the carbon yield to a TOC value.
params.y_toc: Organic-carbon spectroscopy yield attributed to TOC.

toc_from_yield.returns: Total organic carbon TOC = calib*Y_TOC, in the units set by the calibration factor (typically weight fraction or wt%); numpy float64 array.

params.lengths: Transport or migration lengths L_i of the components, e.g. in cm.
params.vol_fracs: Volume fractions v_i of the mixture components (0-1).

transport_length_mix.returns: Harmonic-mean mixture transport/migration length L = 1/sum(v_i/L_i), in the units of lengths; numpy float64. The sum reduces over all elements, so 1-D inputs yield a single value.

params.fy2w: Yield-to-weight conversion factor FY2W for the element.
params.s: Spectral sensitivity factor S for the element.

weights_to_yields.returns: Relative spectroscopy yield Y = W/(FY2W*S), the inverse of yields_to_weights; numpy float64 array.

params.fy2w: Yield-to-weight conversion factor FY2W for the element.
params.s: Spectral sensitivity factor S for the element.
params.y: Measured relative elemental spectroscopy yield Y.

yields_to_weights.returns: Elemental weight fraction W = FY2W*S*Y, dimensionless; numpy float64 array.

params.p1: Initial gas pressure before expansion, in any pressure unit consistent with p2 (only the ratio p1/p2 is used).
params.p2: Equilibrium gas pressure after expansion into the sample cell, in the same unit as p1.
params.v_cell: Volume of the sample cell of the double-cell helium porosimeter, in any volume unit consistent with v_expansion.
params.v_expansion: Volume of the expansion (reference) cell, in the same unit as v_cell.

boyle_grain_volume.returns: Grain volume V_grain = V_cell - V_exp/(p1/p2 - 1), in the same volume unit as the cell volumes. Numpy float64 array broadcast over the inputs.

params.rho_bulk: Bulk density of the sample, in any density unit consistent with rho_grain (g/cc in the corpus).
params.rho_grain: Grain density of the sample, in the same unit as rho_bulk.

boyle_porosity.returns: Boyle's-law density porosity phi = 1 - rho_bulk/rho_grain as a v/v fraction. Numpy float64 array broadcast over the inputs.

params.phi: Porosity (v/v fraction).
params.rho_fl: Pore-fluid density in the same unit as rho_ma; default 1.0.
params.rho_k: Kerogen density in the same unit as the other densities; default 1.30.
params.rho_ma: Matrix (grain) density, in g/cc or kg/m3 (units must be consistent across all densities).
params.v_k: Kerogen volume fraction (v/v); default 0.0 reduces the model to the two-component matrix-plus-fluid form.

bulk_density.returns: Forward-modelled bulk density rho_b = (1-phi-v_k)*rho_ma + v_k*rho_k + phi*rho_fl, in the common density unit. Numpy float64 array broadcast over the inputs.

params.phi: Porosity (v/v fraction).
params.sw: Water saturation as a fraction of pore space (v/v).

bulk_volume_water.returns: Bulk volume water BVW = phi*Sw (v/v fraction of bulk rock), constant within a Buckles zone. Numpy float64 array broadcast over the inputs.

params.*vcl: One or more clay-volume indicator arrays (v/v fractions), all of the same shape, to be combined elementwise.
params.how: Combination rule: 'mean' averages the indicators, 'min' takes the elementwise minimum (most optimistic), 'max' the elementwise maximum (upper bound).

combine_clay_indicators.returns: Combined clay-volume estimate (v/v fraction) with the shape of the input indicators. Numpy float64 array; raises ValueError for an unknown how.

params.clip: Clip range (lo, hi) applied to the porosity; default None (unclipped).
params.mu: Measured CT attenuation of the sample or voxel, in any consistent attenuation unit (e.g. CT number).
params.mu_fluid: CT attenuation of the pure-fluid (100 percent porosity) endpoint, same unit as mu.
params.mu_grain: CT attenuation of the pure-grain (zero-porosity) endpoint, same unit as mu.

ct_porosity.returns: Porosity phi = (mu_grain-mu)/(mu_grain-mu_fluid) as a v/v fraction. Numpy float64 array broadcast over the inputs.

params.clip: Clip range (lo, hi) applied to the saturation; default (0.0, 1.0), pass None to disable.
params.mu: Measured CT attenuation of the partially saturated state, in any consistent attenuation unit.
params.mu_dry: CT attenuation of the dry (zero-saturation) state, same unit as mu.
params.mu_sat: CT attenuation of the fully saturated state, same unit as mu.

ct_saturation.returns: Fluid saturation S = (mu-mu_dry)/(mu_sat-mu_dry) as a v/v fraction, clipped to [0,1] by default. Numpy float64 array broadcast over the inputs.

params.clip: Clip range (lo, hi) applied to the porosity; default None (unclipped, clipping is opt-in).
params.rho_b: Bulk density log values in g/cc.
params.rho_fl: Pore-fluid density endpoint in g/cc; defaults to fresh water 1.0 (brines 1.01-1.03 are passed explicitly).
params.rho_ma: Matrix (grain) density endpoint in g/cc; defaults to sandstone 2.65 (carbonate 2.71 and dolomite 2.85 are passed explicitly).

density_porosity.returns: Density porosity phi_D = (rho_ma-rho_b)/(rho_ma-rho_fl) as a v/v fraction. Numpy float64 array broadcast over the inputs.

params.clip: Clip range (lo, hi) applied to phi_e; default (0.0, None) clips to non-negative, pass None for the unclipped structural-shale-porosity form.
params.phi_sh: Total porosity of the shale endpoint (v/v); explicit, corpus values include 0.10, 0.27, and 0.30.

effective_porosity.returns: Shale-corrected effective porosity phi_e = phi_t - Vsh*phi_sh (v/v fraction), non-negative by default. Numpy float64 array broadcast over the inputs.

params.a: Multiplicative calibration constant; default 1.0704 (the standard tool calibration).
params.b: Additive calibration constant in g/cc; default -0.1883 (the standard tool calibration).
params.rho_e: Electron density from the density tool, in g/cc.

electron_density_to_bulk.returns: Calibrated bulk density rho_b = a*rho_e + b in g/cc. Numpy float64 array broadcast over the input.

params.rhos: Phase densities in g/cc (or any consistent density unit), aligned with saturations along the last axis.
params.saturations: Phase saturations (fractions of pore space, v/v), with the phases along the last axis.

fluid_density.returns: Saturation-weighted pore-fluid density rho_fl = sum(S_i*rho_i), reduced along the last axis, in the unit of rhos. Numpy float64 array.

params.bv_oil: Bulk-volume oil fraction from fluid summation (v/v).

fluid_summation_porosity.returns: Fluid-summation porosity phi = BV_oil + BV_water + BV_gas as a v/v fraction. Numpy float64 array broadcast over the inputs.

params.clip: Clip range (lo, hi) applied to the index; default (0.0, 1.0), pass None to leave it unclipped.
params.gr: Measured gamma-ray log values, in any consistent GR unit (API or GAPI).
params.gr_clean: Gamma-ray reading of the clean (shale-free) endpoint, in the same unit as gr.
params.gr_shale: Gamma-ray reading of the 100 percent shale endpoint, in the same unit as gr.

gamma_ray_index.returns: Dimensionless gamma-ray shale index IGR = (GR-GR_clean)/(GR_shale-GR_clean), clipped to [0,1] by default. Numpy float64 array broadcast over the inputs.

params.m_dry: Dry sample mass, in a mass unit consistent with rho_fluid and v_bulk (the corpus uses g with g/cc and cm3, or kg with kg/m3 and m3).
params.m_sat: Fully fluid-saturated sample mass, in the same unit as m_dry.
params.rho_fluid: Density of the saturating fluid (default 1.0), in units consistent with the masses and bulk volume.
params.v_bulk: Bulk volume of the sample, in a volume unit consistent with the masses and rho_fluid.

gravimetric_porosity.returns: Mass-balance porosity phi = (m_sat-m_dry)/(rho_fluid*V_bulk) as a v/v fraction. Numpy float64 array broadcast over the inputs.

params.phi: Porosity (v/v fraction).
params.sw: Water saturation as a fraction of pore space (v/v).

hydrocarbon_pore_volume.returns: Hydrocarbon pore volume fraction HCPV = phi*(1-Sw) (v/v fraction of bulk rock). Numpy float64 array broadcast over the inputs.

params.depth: Depth values of the samples, in any length unit; irregular sampling is handled via the midpoint gradient.
params.flag: Boolean mask selecting the samples whose thickness contributions are summed.

interval_thickness.returns: Scalar float: summed thickness sum(|dz|) over flagged samples, in the length unit of depth, where dz is the absolute midpoint gradient of depth (a single-sample input uses dz=1).

params.k: Kerogen-to-carbon conversion factor (the kerogen carbon fraction is 1/k); default 1.2, with 1.25 used by some sources.
params.toc: Total organic carbon content; the output carries the same unit (mass fraction or wt%).

kerogen_mass_fraction.returns: Organic-matter (kerogen) mass fraction OM = k*TOC, in the same unit as toc. Numpy float64 array broadcast over the input.

params.carbon_frac: Carbon mass fraction of kerogen; default 0.80.
params.rho_ref: Reference density in g/cc: the bulk density in most source models, or the matrix density in the TMALI form.
params.toc: Total organic carbon as a mass fraction (divide wt% by 100 before passing).

kerogen_volume_from_toc.returns: Kerogen volume fraction V_k = (TOC/carbon_frac)*rho_ref/rho_k (v/v fraction). Numpy float64 array broadcast over the inputs.

params.endpoints: Endpoint response values of each component, as an (n_logs, n_minerals) matrix or a length-n_minerals vector, matrix-multiplied with volumes.
params.volumes: Mineral/component volume fractions vector of length n_minerals (v/v).

log_response.returns: Synthesized tool response(s) M = endpoints @ volumes, in the units of the endpoint values (e.g. API for matrix GR, barns/e for PEF). Numpy float64 array (scalar-like for a single log).

params.rho: Grain densities of the components in g/cc, aligned with w along the last axis.
params.w: Mineral weight (mass) fractions, with the components along the last axis.
params.w_kerogen: Optional kerogen mass fraction added as an extra harmonic term (default 0.0).

matrix_density_from_masses.returns: Mass-weighted (harmonic) matrix density 1/(sum(w_i/rho_i) + w_kerogen/rho_kerogen) in g/cc, reduced along the last axis. Numpy float64 array.

params.rho: Grain densities of the components in g/cc, aligned with v along the last axis.
params.v: Mineral volume fractions (v/v), with the components along the last axis.

matrix_density_from_volumes.returns: Volume-weighted (arithmetic) grain density sum(v_i*rho_i) in g/cc, reduced along the last axis. Numpy float64 array.

params.closure: If True (default), appends a unity-sum row weighted by closure_weight so the volumes obey sum(v)=1; not used by the simplex method, which enforces the constraint by projection.
params.closure_weight: Weight of the appended unity-sum closure row; default 1e3 (some corpus copies use 100).
params.endpoints: Tool response matrix of shape (n_logs, n_minerals): each column holds one mineral's endpoint response on every log.
params.measured: Vector of measured log responses, one value per tool (length n_logs), in each tool's own unit.
params.method: Solver: 'lstsq' (unconstrained least squares, default), 'nnls' (non-negative least squares, lazily imports scipy), or 'simplex' (projected-gradient descent onto v>=0 with sum(v)=1).
params.nonneg: If True, clips a 'lstsq' solution to be non-negative; it has no effect for the 'nnls' or 'simplex' methods.
params.sigma: Optional per-log uncertainties (standard deviations); each equation is scaled by 1/sigma, i.e. inverse-variance weighting. None disables weighting.

multimineral_solve.returns: Mineral volume fractions v (length n_minerals, v/v) solving endpoints @ v = measured under the chosen constraints. Numpy float64 array; raises ValueError for an unknown method.

params.depth: Depth values of the samples, in any length unit.
params.gross_flag: Optional boolean mask defining the gross interval (e.g. a reservoir flag); None (default) uses the whole logged interval as gross.
params.net_flag: Boolean mask flagging the net (e.g. pay) samples.

net_to_gross.returns: Scalar float: dimensionless net-to-gross ratio, net thickness divided by gross thickness (both computed with interval_thickness).

params.method: Combination rule: 'rms' gives the gas-corrected root-mean-square sqrt((phi_n**2+phi_d**2)/2) (default); 'mean' the arithmetic average (phi_n+phi_d)/2.

neutron_density_porosity.returns: Combined neutron-density porosity (v/v fraction). Numpy float64 array broadcast over the inputs; raises ValueError for an unknown method.

params.phi: Porosity curve (v/v fraction), tested against phi_cut.
params.phi_cut: Minimum porosity cutoff (v/v); a sample must have phi >= phi_cut. Default 0.08.
params.sw: Optional water saturation curve (v/v); when given, the condition sw <= sw_cut is added, when None it is skipped.
params.sw_cut: Maximum water saturation cutoff (v/v), applied only if sw is given. Default 0.60.
params.vsh: Optional shale volume curve (v/v); when given, the condition vsh <= vsh_cut is added, when None it is skipped.
params.vsh_cut: Maximum shale volume cutoff (v/v), applied only if vsh is given. Default 0.40.

pay_flag.returns: Boolean numpy array, True where the sample passes all supplied cutoffs (net pay), False otherwise.

params.v_bulk: Sample bulk volume, in any volume unit consistent with v_grain; note this is the first argument (fixed order).
params.v_grain: Sample grain (solid) volume, in the same unit as v_bulk.

porosity_from_volumes.returns: Core porosity phi = (V_bulk - V_grain)/V_bulk as a v/v fraction. Numpy float64 array broadcast over the inputs.

params.pore_voxels: Number of voxels segmented as pore (counted under the caller's own segmentation convention).
params.total_voxels: Total number of voxels in the image volume.

porosity_from_voxel_count.returns: Digital-rock porosity phi = pore_voxels/total_voxels as a v/v fraction. Numpy float64 array broadcast over the inputs.

params.phi_sand: Porosity of the clean sand end member (v/v).
params.phi_sh: Porosity of the shale end member (v/v).
params.v_disp: Dispersed (pore-filling) shale volume fraction subtracted from the porosity; default 0.0 (purely laminated trend).

thomas_stieber_phit.returns: Thomas-Stieber total porosity phi_t = phi_sand*(1-V_lam) + phi_sh*V_lam - V_disp (v/v fraction). Numpy float64 array broadcast over the inputs.

thomas_stieber_sand_porosity.returns: Sand (net) porosity phi_sand = (phi_t - V_lam*phi_sh)/(1 - V_lam) (v/v fraction). Numpy float64 array broadcast over the inputs.

params.clip: Clip range (lo, hi) applied to V_lam; default None (unclipped).

thomas_stieber_vlam.returns: Laminar shale volume V_lam = (phi_sand-phi_t)/(phi_sand-phi_sh) (v/v fraction); the net-to-grain fraction is 1 - V_lam. Numpy float64 array broadcast over the inputs.

params.k_overlay: Overlay coefficient matching the curve type: +0.02 for sonic (default), -2.50 for density, +4.0 for neutron, +0.01 for GR.
params.lom: Level of organic metamorphism (thermal maturity) used in the Passey maturity term; default 10.0.
params.overlay: Overlay porosity-log curve: sonic (us/ft), density (g/cc), neutron (v/v), or GR (API), matching k_overlay.
params.overlay_base: Baseline value of the overlay curve in the lean interval, in the same unit as overlay.
params.rt_base: Baseline resistivity in the non-source (lean) interval, in ohm-m.

toc_passey_dlogr.returns: Passey Delta-log-R total organic carbon, TOC = dlogR * 10**(2.297 - 0.1688*LOM), conventionally in weight percent. Numpy float64 array broadcast over the inputs.

params.a: Numerator constant of the Schmoker correlation; default 154.497 (Schmoker-Hester), other basins pass their own.
params.b: Offset constant of the Schmoker correlation; default 57.261 (Schmoker-Hester).

toc_schmoker.returns: Total organic carbon in weight percent, TOC = a/rho_b - b. Numpy float64 array broadcast over the input.

params.rho: Component densities (g/cc, or any consistent unit), aligned with v along the last axis.
params.v: Volume fractions of the components (v/v), along the last axis.

volume_to_weight_fractions.returns: Weight fractions w_i = v_i*rho_i/sum(v*rho), normalised to sum to one along the last axis. Numpy float64 array with the input shape.

params.clip: Clip range (lo, hi) applied to the final Vsh; default None (unclipped, the corpus Larionov behaviour); the internal IGR is always clipped to [0,1].
params.gcur: Exponent of the generalized 'stieber_gcur' transform (2**(gcur*IGR)-1)/(2**gcur-1); default 2.0, ignored by the other methods.
params.gr: Measured gamma-ray log values, in any consistent GR unit (API or GAPI); pass an already-computed IGR with gr_clean=0 and gr_shale=1.
params.gr_clean: Gamma-ray reading of the clean (shale-free) endpoint, in the same unit as gr.
params.gr_shale: Gamma-ray reading of the 100 percent shale endpoint, in the same unit as gr.
params.method: Vsh transform: 'linear' (Vsh=IGR), 'larionov_tertiary', 'larionov_older', 'clavier', 'steiber', or 'stieber_gcur' (the normalised generalized-Stieber form using gcur).

vshale_from_gr.returns: Shale volume fraction Vsh (v/v, dimensionless) from the selected gamma-ray-index transform. Numpy float64 array broadcast over the inputs; raises ValueError for an unknown method.

params.clip: Clip range (lo, hi) applied to the resulting Vsh; default None (unclipped).
params.phi_d_sh: Density porosity at the shale point (v/v fraction).
params.phi_n_sh: Neutron porosity at the shale point (v/v fraction).

vshale_neutron_density.returns: Shale volume fraction (v/v): the local neutron-density separation divided by the shale-point separation. Numpy float64 array broadcast over the inputs.

params.rho: Component densities (g/cc, or any consistent unit), aligned with w along the last axis.
params.w: Weight (mass) fractions of the components, along the last axis.

weight_to_volume_fractions.returns: Volume fractions v_i = (w_i/rho_i)/sum(w/rho), normalised to sum to one along the last axis. Numpy float64 array with the input shape.

params.vo_forced: Volume of oil displaced by forced drainage (or the equivalent saturation change), same units.
params.vo_spont: Volume of oil displaced spontaneously (or the equivalent saturation change), same units.
params.vw_forced: Volume of water imbibed under forced displacement (or the equivalent saturation change), same units.
params.vw_spont: Volume of water imbibed spontaneously (or the equivalent saturation change), any consistent volume unit.

amott_indices.returns: Tuple of three floats (Iw, Io, Iah): the water index Vw_spont/(Vw_spont+Vw_forced), the oil index likewise, and the Amott-Harvey index Iah = Iw - Io; each ratio is zero-guarded (0.0 when its total is not positive).

params.drho: Density difference between the two fluids, kg/m3 with SI inputs.
params.k: Characteristic area: permeability in m2 for the Darcy form, or a squared pore radius (pass k = R**2) for the pore-radius form.
params.sigma: Interfacial tension between the fluids, N/m with SI inputs.

bond_number.returns: Bond number Nb = drho*g*k/sigma, the gravity-to-capillary force ratio, dimensionless, as a numpy float64 array.

params.clip: Tuple (lo, hi) that clips the normalized saturation Se, default (0.0, 1.0); None disables clipping.
params.lam: Brooks-Corey pore-size-distribution index lambda, dimensionless, which fixes the Burdine exponents (2+3*lam)/lam and (2+lam)/lam.
params.snwr: Residual non-wetting-phase saturation, fraction, subtracted in the Se denominator, default 0.
params.sw: Wetting-phase (water) saturation, as a fraction of pore volume.
params.swr: Residual wetting-phase saturation, fraction, used in the Se normalization.

brooks_corey_burdine_kr.returns: Tuple (krw, krnw) of numpy float64 arrays: wetting-phase kr = Se**((2+3*lam)/lam) and non-wetting kr = (1-Se)**2 * (1 - Se**((2+lam)/lam)), both dimensionless with endpoint 1 (no kr_max factor).

params.exponent: Steepness exponent of the transition, default 1.0; articles writing it as 1/width pass exponent = 1.0/width.
params.n: Trapping (or capillary) number at which to evaluate the residual saturation, dimensionless.
params.n_crit: Critical trapping number at the midpoint of the desaturation transition, dimensionless.
params.sor_max: Plateau residual oil saturation at low trapping numbers, fraction of pore volume.
params.sor_min: Floor residual saturation approached at high trapping numbers, fraction of pore volume.

capillary_desaturation.returns: Residual oil saturation Sor(N) = Sor_min + (Sor_max - Sor_min)/(1 + (N/N_crit)**exponent), a fraction falling from sor_max toward sor_min, as a numpy float64 array.

params.unit: Output slowness unit selector: us/ft (default) or us/m; any other value raises ValueError.

velocity_to_slowness.returns: Sonic slowness as a numpy float64 array, in us/ft or us/m according to unit.

params.azi1: Azimuth at the first survey station, in degrees.
params.azi2: Azimuth at the second survey station, in degrees.
params.inc1: Inclination at the first survey station, in degrees.
params.inc2: Inclination at the second survey station, in degrees.

dogleg_angle.returns: Dogleg (total curvature) angle between the two stations as a scalar Python float in radians, from the spherical-law-of-cosines rearrangement with the cosine clipped to [-1, 1] before arccos.

params.inc_deg: Array of inclinations in degrees, paired with md.
params.md: Array of measured depths at the survey stations.
params.method: Conversion convention: "min_curvature" (default) uses the azimuth-independent balanced minimum-curvature vertical increments; "tangential" uses the legacy single-inclination form summing dMD*cos(inc) with each segment's lower-station inclination; other values raise ValueError.

md_to_tvd.returns: Cumulative true vertical depth as a numpy float64 array of the same length as md, in the length units of md with TVD positive downward.

params.azi1: Azimuth at the first station, in degrees.
params.azi2: Azimuth at the second station, in degrees.
params.inc1: Inclination at the first station, in degrees.
params.inc2: Inclination at the second station, in degrees.
params.md1: Measured depth at the first survey station; its length unit sets the unit of the returned increments.
params.md2: Measured depth at the second station, in the same length units as md1.

minimum_curvature_step.returns: Tuple (dTVD, dNorth, dEast) of Python floats: the balanced minimum-curvature displacement increments between the two stations, in the length units of md, with TVD positive downward.

params.dogleg_rad: Dogleg angle between the two survey stations, in radians.

ratio_factor.returns: Minimum-curvature ratio (radius) factor RF = (2/DL)*tan(DL/2) as a scalar Python float; exactly 1.0 for a near-straight step (DL below 1e-9), the balanced-tangential limit.

params.azi: Array of azimuths at the stations, in degrees, parallel to md.
params.inc: Array of inclinations at the stations, in degrees, parallel to md.
params.md: Array of measured depths at consecutive survey stations; its length unit sets the trajectory units.

survey_to_path.returns: Numpy float64 array of shape (n, 3): the cumulative minimum-curvature trajectory integrated from the origin (0, 0, 0), with column order (TVD, North, East) in the length units of md and TVD positive downward.
